Chromium Daily Summaries

Latest Chromium commits summarized daily

📅 View Weekly Summaries →

2026-05-24

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-24

Overview

This digest covers 11 commits from (c7d5c34) to (93ba0ac). After filtering for changes relevant to embedders on desktop platforms, 7 commits were analyzed. This report focuses on updates to UI, Security, Networking, Media, and Infrastructure. No major breaking changes were identified in this set of commits.

Security & Identity

  • As part of the ongoing work for Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC), a new method, GenerateTokenBindingRegistrationToken(), has been added to the internal ProfileOAuth2TokenService interface. This is a preparatory step to expose token generation through the public IdentityManager API in the future. (d309566)
    SecurityAPIProfiles
  • The DBSC token helper state is now cleared when the last binding key is removed (e.g., on sign-out) to prevent key reuse after a user signs out and back in. As part of this, UnexportableKeyLoader was updated to correctly cancel pending callbacks upon its destruction. (e071a25)
    SecurityProfiles

Browser & UI

  • A prototype state synchronization mechanism has been added for the experimental "Full WebUI Omnibox" popup. This change allows input state to be preserved across tab switches but is explicitly a prototype and not yet a stable feature. (8cc4dee)
    UIBrowser

Media

  • The third-party AV1 codec library, libaom, has been updated to version 3.14.1. This roll includes a fix for checking bitstream buffer size. (93ba0ac)
    Media
  • Tracing in the media/cast component has been refactored to replace asynchronous track events with more appropriate synchronous or flow-connected events, improving debugging and performance analysis. (7853374)
    MediaDevTools

Networking

  • The list of pervasive resource patterns, used by the network service to identify common third-party resources, has received its monthly update from the HTTP Archive. (c7d5c34)
    Network

Infrastructure

  • String resources for various languages (e.g., ca, hi, ja, mn) have been updated across the codebase. (717e501)
    I18nInfra

2026-05-23

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-23

Overview

This digest covers 52 commits from May 21st to May 22nd, 2026, spanning from commit b3ed2f78 to 33b8638b. After filtering for changes relevant to desktop browser development, this report highlights key updates in Security, UI, Extensions, and core browser infrastructure. Notable changes include the removal of the experimental Compute Pressure API, significant security hardening in the Web Request API and macOS Bluetooth implementation, and ongoing development of the new WebUI-based toolbar.

Security

  • Fixed a critical cross-origin secret leak in the Extensions Web Request API. A compromised renderer could cause request ID collisions, leading to network callbacks being routed to the wrong request. The fix now detects collisions, aborts the request, and terminates the offending renderer process to prevent data leakage. (048923c)
    SecurityExtensionsNetwork
  • The experimental Compute Pressure API has been removed due to an information leak vulnerability. The feature was not in active development and is now fully reverted. (4d118b5) (d235c83) (a8f43ad) (448f7cd)
    SecurityPrivacyBlink
  • Addressed a use-after-free vulnerability in Bluetooth socket listeners on macOS. The fix prevents a crash that could occur when the OS framework dispatched a late callback to a freed listener object after unregistration. (375096a)
    SecurityBluetoothmacOS
  • Improved password redaction in screenshots by leveraging the TrackedElement infrastructure. This allows for more accurate identification and masking of sensitive password fields in captured viewport bitmaps. (421393c)
    SecurityPrivacyUI

User Interface

  • As part of the experimental WebUI Toolbar, an extensions container has been added. While not yet connected to the UI, this lays the groundwork for rendering extension actions in the new toolbar. (1b16115)
    UIExtensions
  • The security icon in the WebUI-based location bar has been refactored to use a native <button> element. This improves accessibility by ensuring screen readers can properly announce the security status. (b889e37)
    UIAccessibilitySecurity
  • Several icons have been updated to the new rounded 'Glow Up' style as part of the ongoing UI modernization effort, affecting elements like the browser_actions menu and autofill popups. (f39794b)
    UIThemes
  • Fixed a bug where the page info icon in the location bar would animate incorrectly on internal pages like chrome://settings. The animation is now correctly suppressed when the icon is updated for non-page-info contexts. (a88841b)
    UIBrowser
  • Long descriptions in the PWA installation dialog are now elided to prevent layout issues, aligning the behavior with legacy installation prompts. (6dddd7a)
    UIBrowser

Browser Internals & Infrastructure

  • The build system now supports TypeScript v7 via a new use_typescript_go GN argument. This modernizes the toolchain for developers working on WebUI components. (1487cbd)
    BuildInfraUI
  • The History Embeddings feature can now process PDFs, allowing their titles and URLs to be included as candidates for semantic history search. (23aebd5)
    HistoryPDFAI
  • Fixed a threading violation in the Cast Mirroring Service by ensuring the VideoCaptureHost Mojo receiver is consistently accessed on the I/O thread. (47ea02f)
    MediaNetworkBrowser
  • The WebUI Lit Template Formatter tool (lit_template_formatter) now includes a --dry-run flag to check for formatting issues without modifying files, improving its integration with git cl format. (c3fe194)
    BuildDevToolsInfra
  • Improved the reliability of browser tests for the PDF Ink Signatures feature by fixing several race conditions and assertion failures related to text box initialization and asynchronous plugin messages. (c9db5bb)
    PDFTestingBrowser

2026-05-22

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-22

Overview

This summary covers 703 commits on the main branch for May 22, 2026, with 507 identified as relevant to developers. Key changes include major API refactorings, the finalization of Manifest V2 deprecation steps, and significant updates to the build system. View the full log from (e5b651c) to (c14e0f1).

Extensions

  • ⚠️A BREAKING CHANGE as the kExtensionManifestV2Disabled feature flag has been removed. This forces all Manifest V2 extensions into the "disable" phase, removing the intermediate "warning" stage. Embedders relying on this flag to delay the transition must update. (e768ae9)
    ExtensionsBrowser
  • Security for extension background pages was hardened with per-frame deduplication and strict activity matching for lazy keepalive decrements, preventing premature termination by a compromised renderer. (4a7596d)
    ExtensionsSecurity
  • Fixed a null-dereference crash in the bookmarkManagerPrivate.drop API. The code now safely handles cases where drag data is missing, preventing a potential browser crash. (9d324e9)
    ExtensionsBookmarks

Browser & Core APIs

  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: The core Tab Strip service API has been moved from chrome/browser/ui/tabs/tab_strip_api to a new component at components/browser_apis/tab_strip. This changes build dependencies and header include paths for embedders using this public API. (d10436b)
    BrowserTabsAPI
  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: The public method AccountTrackerService::Initialize has been removed. Its logic is now in the constructor, requiring PrefService* and base::FilePath on instantiation. Embedders must update to a single-step initialization. (c0a736f)
    SyncProfilesAPI
  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: The public method Browser::is_delete_scheduled() has been removed. Code must be updated to use BrowserWindowInterface::IsDeleteScheduled() instead. (1353217)
    BrowserAPI
  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: The behavior of WebContents::GetFocusedWebContents() has changed. It can now return nullptr in scenarios involving embedded WebContents, which may cause null pointer dereferences in embedder code that previously assumed a non-null return value. (1ad0580)
    BrowserAPIUI
  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: The base::FileTracing::Provider interface has been removed. File tracing is now handled internally within base::File using Perfetto, removing the public API for supplying a custom file tracing provider. (6855e06)
    BaseInfra
  • A new IcuBridge class has been introduced in base/i18n to abstract away direct calls to the ICU library. This is in preparation for supporting different ICU backends. (d829192)
    BaseI18n

Build System

  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: The build system for WebUI has been significantly modularized. Files from the monolithic //chrome/browser/ui:ui target have been moved to new, smaller source sets (e.g., //chrome/browser/ui/webui/). Build systems with direct dependencies on old file locations will break. (9694359)
    BuildUIInfra
  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: fuzzable_proto_library targets now produce shared libraries (components) instead of static libraries in component builds. This change, made to fix ODR violations, will break custom build configurations that relied on static linking. (0fcbba9)
    BuildTestingInfra

UI & UX

  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: The components/eye_dropper/EyeDropperView has been refactored to use a controller pattern and no longer inherits from views::WidgetDelegateView. This changes the public API's ownership model and structure, breaking embedders that use this component. (23a8d51)
    UIAPI
  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: Support for the "borderless" value for the display manifest member and display-mode CSS media feature has been removed. It was an alias for "unframed", which must now be used exclusively. (8df25b6)
    UICSS
  • A new feature for global Most-Recently-Used (MRU) tab switching with Ctrl-Tab has been implemented. Controlled by the kCtrlTabMru flag, it allows users to toggle between the two most recent tabs across all windows. (2e673d0)
    UITabsBrowser
  • On modern macOS keyboards, the text placeholder (fn) for function key shortcuts is now replaced with the proper monochrome globe symbol (🌐︎), aligning Chrome's UI with native platform appearance. (9917a2e)
    UImacOS
  • The tab loading throbber animation is now compositor-driven, eliminating a repeating timer to improve rendering performance. This may result in a slight visual change to the animation's 'sweeping arc'. (cca3271)
    UIPerformanceCompositor

Graphics & Printing

  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: For security hardening, MapBufferRange, UnmapBuffer, and FlushMappedBufferRange have been removed from the public gpu::gles2::GLES2Interface. This breaks embedders who use this low-level graphics interface directly. (7cc1fc6)
    GPUAPISecurity
  • ⚠️BREAKING CHANGE: The printing::mojom::PrintManagerHost Mojo interface has been refactored. UpdatePrintSettings was renamed to GetPrintPreviewParams and its method signature was altered, breaking the IPC contract for print preview. (ea38e74)
    PrintingAPI

Networking & Security

  • A new option, server_padding, has been added to net::SSLServerConfig. This allows embedders to enable TLS server-side padding as a defense against traffic analysis. (6eb3ab2)
    NetworkSecuritySSL
  • On Windows, support has been added to force Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR) for sandboxed child processes, making them harder to exploit. Embedders can override this via the new ContentBrowserClient::ShouldForceASLR() method. (09eaeae)
    SecurityWindows
  • Download path validation was updated to prevent symlink traversal attacks by ensuring the final resolved file path is strictly contained within the intended download directory. (3cf6610)
    SecurityDownloads
  • Fixed a use-after-free crash in the ProxyResolver service that could occur when multiple resolvers shared a V8 isolate. The fix ensures stale cleanup tasks are properly pruned. (69cbd06)
    NetworkV8

Autofill

  • As part of the Email Verification Protocol (EVP) feature, the AutofillClient interface was updated with a new ShowEmailVerificationPopup method to display a permission prompt before sharing a verification token. (e4c8c19)
    AutofillUI

Testing

  • The 'Kombucha' interactive testing framework was updated to support non-blocking mouse gestures. Test APIs like MoveMouseTo now use a callback chain instead of nested run loops, a significant change for authors of interactive UI tests. (735d345)
    TestingUI
  • New GTest matchers (FormFieldDataEq, FormDataEq) were introduced for comparing Autofill data structures. They provide more detailed property-level mismatch messages, improving the clarity of test failures. (0611378)
    TestingAutofill

2026-05-21

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-21

Overview

This summary covers 529 relevant commits out of a total of 711, from commit def4c1989479 to 4492467c1c34. The day saw an unusually high number of breaking changes affecting core embedder APIs, WebAuthn, Autofill, and Networking. Other notable work includes security hardening for PWAs and WebNN, UI improvements for split tabs and theming, and performance optimizations in the disk cache.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️MAJOR: The core content::ContentBrowserClient interface has a new pure virtual method, GetContainerPolicyOverrideForCommit, which must be implemented by embedders. This allows overriding a frame's container policy at commit time. (6da6b23)
    BrowserSecurityExtensions
  • ⚠️The AutofillManager::Observer interface signature has changed. The OnFillOrPreviewForm method now includes a new trigger_field_id parameter, requiring updates to all existing observer implementations. (83568b9)
    AutofillUI
  • ⚠️The base::MemoryConsumerRegistry public class has changed the signature of its protected virtual methods OnMemoryConsumerAdded and OnMemoryConsumerRemoved. They now pass a raw MemoryConsumer* pointer instead of a wrapper, requiring embedders who subclass this to update their overrides. (339a249)
    BasePerformance
  • ⚠️The public static method net::CookiePartitionKeyCollection::Todo() has been removed. Callers must now use the default constructor net::CookiePartitionKeyCollection() instead. (1dfabc4)
    NetworkPrivacy
  • ⚠️The WebAuthn API surface has been reduced. The chrome/browser/webauthn/cablev2_devices.h header and several unused public methods and type aliases from WebAuthn UI headers (e.g., HoverListView) have been removed. (28f957a) (368d594)
    WebAuthnSecurityUI
  • ⚠️The WebXR presentation API XRPresentationProvider::SubmitFrameDrawnIntoTexture has a new signature. It now accepts a vector of XRLayerUpdatePtr instead of separate layer IDs and a single SyncToken, impacting custom WebXR implementations. (a1ff25b)
    XRGPU
  • ⚠️The remoting/host API DesktopDisplayInfoMonitor::AddCallback now returns a base::CallbackListSubscription instead of void. Consumers must store this subscription to manage the callback's lifetime. (5fbcbfa)
    NetworkUIBase
  • ⚠️The MediaDevices.setPreferredSinkId() web API is now restricted to top-level documents. Calls from iframes will now throw a DOMException, a behavioral break for web apps using this in embedded contexts. (1e43d45)
    MediaBlinkSecurity

APIs and Extensions

  • A new pure virtual method, AllowlistStylesheet(), has been added to the public blink::WebDocumentSubresourceFilter interface. Embedders who implement this interface must add an implementation for this new method. (553277f)
    BlinkSecurity
  • Several use-after-free vulnerabilities in the extension ProcessManager have been fixed. These occurred when closing lazy background pages that had open views or dialogs, and are resolved by safely managing WebContents destruction. (035bd21) (e1de04b)
    ExtensionsSecurity
  • The BindingKeyRegistrationTokenHelper has been moved from chrome/browser/signin to the more accessible components/signin/public/base to reduce layering violations and improve modularity. (5fc0930) (d81053f) (06b35b9)
    ProfilesSyncBase

Security and Privacy

  • The WebAppInstallForceList enterprise policy now enforces icon hash validation during installation. This prevents a potential script execution vulnerability by securely downloading and verifying icons in the browser process. (75d3397)
    EnterpriseSecurityBrowser
  • The WebNN API is being hardened by moving graph validation and compilation into a separate utility process. New mojom interfaces (WebNNCompilerService, WebNNCompilerContext) define this new security boundary. (a1a5ab0)
    AISecurityGPU
  • On-device speech recognition is now gated by the on-device-speech-recognition Permissions-Policy, preventing cross-origin iframes from using the feature without explicit permission. (5d69e4c)
    AISecurityPrivacy
  • The file quarantine check for saved pages on Windows and macOS now correctly uses the final destination URL after redirects, ensuring security checks are applied to the correct source. (9c86d91)
    SecurityDownloads

Browser UI and Features

  • The session restore service has been updated to correctly restore split tab layouts and tab groups within them after a browser restart or crash. (a39bde7) (2897e0b)
    TabsBrowserUI
  • On macOS, a bug where keyboard events could be sent to the wrong window after a focus change has been fixed. The CommandDispatcher now verifies the window is still key before redispatching events. (eee6356)
    macOSUI
  • The BubbleFrameView was updated to natively support rasterizing vector icons, fixing a bug where icons would not update their color on system theme changes (e.g., switching to dark mode). (d177665)
    UIThemes
  • An experimental, next-generation Omnibox popup is being developed under the kWebUIOmniboxFullPopupV2 feature flag. This version embeds the WebUI popup directly into the BrowserView to improve window management. (cecf7a8)
    UIBrowser

Networking and Performance

  • To reduce memory usage, the SQL disk cache's in-memory index no longer stores a res_id_to_hash_map_, saving 10-20 bytes per cache entry. This was achieved by refactoring callers to provide both hash and resource ID. (5a748bb)
    PerformanceStorageNetwork
  • The Chromium Updater (update_client) now caps the server-instructed backoff delay from the X-Retry-After header at 24 hours. This prevents a misconfigured server from suppressing updates indefinitely. (9925a7c)
    UpdaterNetwork
  • New histograms have been added to monitor WebSocket-over-HTTP/3 session reuse and error codes, providing better diagnostics for this transport. (a5a3f02)
    NetworkPerformance

Build and Internals

  • A new clang plugin for "strict deps" has been implemented to enforce better build dependency hygiene. It provides actionable error messages suggesting gn commands to fix missing dependencies. (79c86cb)
    BuildInfra
  • The build system now integrates TypeScript v7 when the use_typescript_go flag is enabled. (dd56ca1)
    BuildUI
  • A race condition in the Windows-specific WRL module helper has been fixed. The bug could lead to a Use-After-Free when multiple threads drove an object's reference count to zero concurrently. (b30f435)
    BaseWindows

2026-05-20

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-20

Overview

This summary covers 523 relevant commits out of 692 total on the main branch for May 20, 2026, from commit a285b7adff3d... to c354f0a09ec5.... The focus is on changes impacting developers of Chromium-based projects, with a strong emphasis on breaking changes, new APIs, and updates to the browser UI, security, and networking subsystems for Windows and macOS.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public C++ interface RenderFrameHostImpl::AddResourceTimingEntryForFailedSubframeNavigation has a new signature. The method now takes a SubframeResourceLengths struct and a completion_time instead of a URLLoaderCompletionStatus object, breaking embedders who call this method directly. (a6a9a9f)
    BrowserAPINetwork
  • ⚠️Several public helper functions have been removed from chrome/browser/ui/browser_finder.h, including chrome::GetIncognitoBrowserCount() and chrome::GetGuestBrowserCount(). Embedders relying on these functions will need to find alternative ways to query browser state. (686390b)
    BrowserAPI
  • ⚠️The public API media::VideoCaptureDevice::Client::OnIncomingCapturedImage now requires a new const gfx::Size& natural_size parameter. Embedders implementing this client interface for video capture must update their method signatures. (636ff60)
    MediaAPI
  • ⚠️The audio::SoundsManager and audio::AudioStreamHandler APIs have been changed to accept a resource ID (int) instead of raw data (std::string_view). Embedders using this API to play sounds from memory must now package audio as a resource. (2788a58)
    MediaAPI
  • ⚠️The PinnedToolbarAction enum in the public toolbar_ui_api_data_model.mojom interface has been simplified. Several media-related enums are consolidated into kRouteMedia, requiring embedders to pass the icon separately. (a3798e5)
    UIAPI
  • ⚠️The ConfirmInfoBar class is now an abstract base class. Direct instantiation with std::make_unique<ConfirmInfoBar> will fail; use the new static factory method ConfirmInfoBar::Create() instead. (0cda611)
    UIBanner
  • ⚠️The public virtual method ui::TouchEditable::IsCommandIdEnabled has a new bool can_paste parameter. Subclasses must update their implementation to match the new signature. (b57bb78)
    UI
  • ⚠️The initially_focused_view parameter in views::Widget::InitParams has changed from std::optional<View*> to std::unique_ptr<ViewTracker>. Code that directly constructs widgets will need to be updated. (5c153ab)
    UI
  • ⚠️Public enum values in the split_tabs::SplitTabLayout API have been renamed for clarity. kVertical is now kSideBySide and kHorizontal is now kStacked. (4abe9bf)
    UITabs
  • ⚠️The SupervisedUserBlockInterstitialV3 feature flag and the public function supervised_user_error_page::GetBlockMessageV2 have been removed, making the V3 UI permanent. Embedders calling the removed function will have build failures. (4622302)
    BrowserAPI

API & Extensions

  • The DownloadManager interface now includes asynchronous versions of GetAllDownloads() and GetDownload(). This is a non-breaking addition that allows for deferred initialization of the download system to improve startup performance. (8cbf419)
    DownloadsAPIPerformance
  • An experimental embedderData field has been added to TargetInfo in the DevTools protocol. The new DevToolsManagerDelegate::GetEmbedderDataForTarget hook allows embedders to populate this field with custom metadata. (5aa804a)
    DevToolsAPI
  • A new enterprise policy, HttpHeaderInjection, has been defined. It will allow administrators to inject custom HTTP headers into requests for specified URL patterns. The network interceptor implementation is forthcoming. (a6a8295)
    PolicyNetworkEnterprise
  • The WebUI Omnibox now sends the cursor position to the AutocompleteController, enabling more context-aware suggestions, such as completing a word in the middle of a query. (1ad530f)
    UIAPI
  • A cleanup mechanism was added to ExtensionPrefs to deduplicate sub-event filters (e.g., for webRequest) that may have accumulated in user preferences over time. (6e05d4d)
    ExtensionsStorage

UI & Views

  • On Windows, a new feature allows views::Widget instances to be excluded from screen capture via SetExcludeFromScreenCapture(). This is intended for Picture-in-Picture windows. (7d0d0a7)
    UIWindows
  • The revamped "Clear Browsing Data" dialog is now permanent, as the kDbdRevampDesktop feature flag has been removed. Embedders relying on the old UI will now see the new version. (30ea8b7)
    UIPrivacy
  • The Windows system menu can now use a Views-based implementation, controlled by the kMenuSimplification feature flag, as part of ongoing UI modernization efforts. (2ae8ddc) (7aee783)
    UIWindows
  • On macOS, a bug where the decorative "Glass" frame could render on top of web content and intercept clicks has been fixed by correctly sorting the NSGlassEffectView in the view hierarchy. (5dc59c8)
    UImacOS
  • Native UI text rendering on Windows and macOS now correctly honors the VS-15 (U+FE0E) variation selector, forcing emoji-default characters to render in their monochrome text presentation. (4c82c62)
    UIFonts
  • The First Run Experience (FRE) has been updated to include a "Feature Showcase" step to introduce users to new capabilities. (7219a0c)
    UIBrowser

Security & Privacy

  • A potential origin spoofing vulnerability in Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) has been fixed. When a PWA is uninstalled, its windows are now forcibly closed, bypassing beforeunload handlers to prevent the window from remaining open without its custom UI. (d47269c)
    SecurityBrowser
  • On Windows, trust verification for the remote WebAuthn native messaging host has been hardened by using PathService and verifying the binary is signed by Google. (448fb48)
    SecurityWindows
  • The macOS renderer sandbox was hardened to deny the ability to post distributed notifications, controlled by the new kMacSandboxDistributedNotifications feature flag. (cd96f38)
    SecuritymacOS
  • Navigations initiated from the Reading Mode side panel are now more secure. The browser now verifies user gestures and downgrades CURRENT_TAB navigations to NEW_FOREGROUND_TAB to prevent a compromised renderer from hijacking the active tab. (a30257f)
    SecurityNavigation
  • Device-Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) registration will now use the final URL after any redirects to perform validation checks, improving the security of the registration process. (159631e)
    SecurityNetwork

Performance & Infrastructure

  • LazyBrowserInterfaceBroker is now enabled by default, which is expected to save browser memory by deferring the creation of some interfaces. (bdccc71)
    PerformanceBrowser
  • New histograms, Startup.FirstWebContents.FirstContentfulPaint and Startup.FirstWebContents.LargestContentfulPaint, have been added to provide more standardized performance insights for the initial tab. (a05b3e4)
    Performance
  • The HTTP cache initialization process has been updated to detect and delete empty cache directories on startup, reclaiming disk space. (6dff202)
    NetworkStoragePerformance
  • A bug in ZstdSourceStream that could violate the FilterSourceStream contract has been fixed. The stream will no longer return zero output bytes while leaving input unconsumed. (9aba30f)
    Network
  • The [[nodiscard]] attribute has been added to the RenderFrameHost::FrameIterationAction enum to help prevent bugs where its return value is ignored during frame traversal. (64f1be3)
    BaseBrowser

Testing

  • A suite of testing flags for web app install dialogs has been unified into a standard RAII pattern with dedicated helper classes like ScopedAutoAcceptWebAppDialogs, centralizing logic and cleaning up test code. (fff5596)
    TestingBrowser
  • The fuzzer_test build target now supports a dict_deps attribute, allowing fuzzer dictionaries to be generated as part of the build process. (c5f7aef)
    TestingBuild

2026-05-19

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-19

Overview

This digest covers 704 commits on May 19, 2026, with 530 commits filtered as relevant to developers. The changes span from the first commit 57f11432f6cb94138958a5923a980f0d68009f8f to the last d96a1c977af36e723288493f119c68e6672d1914. This summary highlights a significant number of breaking API changes affecting media, UI, networking, and extensions. Developers should pay close attention to signature changes in the core AudioManager, the removal of the FullscreenObserver pattern, and updates to the winhttp and accessibility APIs. Other notable changes include security hardening on Windows and macOS, and the removal of policy support in the headless shell.

Browser & Core APIs

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public content::FrameConnector interface has two new pure virtual methods, SetKeepSurfaceAlive and IsKeepingAlive. Embedders who implement this interface must now provide implementations for these new methods to support capturing background tabs. (9675bd8)
    BrowserUI
  • ⚠️BREAKING: Support for applying Chrome policies in the headless shell has been completely removed. The headless_use_policy GN flag and all associated logic are gone, which will affect embedders who used policies for configuration. (fcdf7e8)
    BrowserPolicy
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The last_active_time_ticks and last_active_elapsed_text properties have been removed from the tabs_api.mojom.Tab Mojo interface. Clients of the TabStrip API using these fields will need to be updated. (5f89e9c)
    BrowserTabsUI
  • The winhttp::NetworkFetcher was refactored to enforce a strict single-use contract to prevent Use-After-Free vulnerabilities. Reusing an instance will now trigger a CHECK failure. (98cf28d)
    NetworkWindowsSecurity
  • A significant build system refactoring was performed on //chrome/browser to break up the monolithic :browser target into smaller, header-only source sets, improving dependency management. (a3e448d)
    BuildBrowser

UI & Accessibility

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The FullscreenObserver class and its associated AddObserver/RemoveObserver methods have been removed from FullscreenController. Consumers must migrate to the new RegisterOnFullscreenStateChanged method, which uses a base::CallbackListSubscription pattern. (00d519b)
    UIBrowser
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The ActionFlags enum and the flags field have been removed from the public ui::AXActionData structure. This is an API-breaking change for embedders using Chromium's accessibility interfaces. (da04fe8)
    AccessibilityUI
  • A new, safer API for creating bubble widgets, views::BubbleDialogDelegateView::CreateBubble, has been introduced. It returns a std::unique_ptr<Widget> to improve memory management and clarify ownership. (0b4629d)
    UI
  • A new method, SetShouldAllowKeyEventsDuringInputProtection, was added to ui::DialogModel::Builder to allow dialogs to block key events like Enter and Esc during the initial input protection period, preventing keyjacking. (e64e0f3)
    UISecurity

Media

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The signatures for AudioManager::GetAudioInputDeviceNames() and GetAudioOutputDeviceNames() have been changed to return a bool indicating success instead of void. This is a major breaking change for any embedder that implements a custom AudioManager or calls these methods directly. (d1b707c)
    Media
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The constructor for media::AudioDiscardHelper has been modified, removing the delayed_discard boolean parameter. This will break custom audio decoders or media pipeline components that instantiate this class. (9877688) (e7794c6)
    Media
  • A security vulnerability in the Media Foundation CDM on Windows was patched to prevent a site isolation bypass by correctly migrating permissions on existing CDM store roots. (4e5395b)
    MediaSecurityWindows

Extensions

  • ⚠️BREAKING: A legacy compatibility layer for upgrading New Tab Page extension permissions has been removed. Extensions relying on the old, automatic upgrade path will now be disabled upon update, requiring developers to update their manifest and installation flow. (81cc594)
    Extensions
  • The default Content Security Policy (CSP) for MV3+ extensions is now also applied to extensions of type kUserScript for greater consistency and security. (6de2ae4)
    ExtensionsSecurity

Networking

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The winhttp::NetworkFetcher completion callback on transport-level failures now receives the exact HRESULT error code instead of an HTTP 200 status. Consumers must update their error handling logic to interpret HRESULT values. (2720391)
    NetworkWindows
  • The stable feature flag kForceSecureDnsDohFallback has been removed. Configurations or embedders relying on this flag to force DNS-over-HTTPS fallback will see a behavioral change. (d495b25)
    NetworkPrivacy
  • Search engines can now opt-in to receiving the X-Geo header by setting a new send_x_geo_header field in their prepopulated engine data, allowing for location-aware search results. (a197dda)
    NetworkBrowser

Security

  • On Windows, PWA installation now sanitizes app names that are reserved device names (e.g., PRN, COM1) to prevent potential denial-of-service or unauthorized device I/O vulnerabilities. (1981552)
    SecurityWindowsBrowser
  • PartitionAlloc hardening, including MiraclePtr, is now correctly enabled in the macOS app-shim process to mitigate potential exploits from untrusted renderer IPCs. (68319a1)
    SecuritymacOSBase
  • A use-after-free vulnerability in LegacyRenderWidgetHostHWND on Windows has been fixed by adding guards against re-entrant calls that could destroy the object while it was still in use. (c50e173)
    SecurityWindowsUI
  • A dangling pointer crash in DevToolsRemoteServerInfobarDelegate has been resolved by fetching the active browser on-demand instead of caching a raw_ptr that could become invalid. (47edc8f)
    SecurityDevToolsBrowser

Testing & Infrastructure

  • A new MojoBrowserProxyFactory class has been added to ui/webui/resources/mojo to simplify the creation of test browser proxies for Mojo-based WebUI, reducing boilerplate code for feature authors. (f500fe8)
    TestingUI
  • The --ignore-gpu-blocklist command-line switch was extended to accept a comma-separated list of entry IDs, allowing for more granular debugging of specific driver workarounds. (6f26990)
    GPUTesting

2026-05-18

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-18

Overview

On 2026-05-18, the Chromium main branch saw 568 commits, with 392 identified as relevant for this summary. This digest covers significant changes from the first commit (de2beb1c7c636bfbb4b118aace8cef6741414676) to the last (f48d54345489b0a960f7f8a7b2bbd9d01ab2951d), focusing on API updates, security, and browser features. The day was marked by numerous breaking changes across core browser, sync, extension, and build systems, requiring attention from developers and embedders.

Browser & Core APIs

  • ⚠️BREAKING: Several widely used public helper functions in chrome/browser/ui/browser_finder.h have been removed, including chrome::FindTabbedBrowser, chrome::FindBrowserWithProfile, and chrome::GetTotalBrowserCount. The function chrome::CloseAllBrowsersWithProfile was also moved to chrome/browser/lifetime/application_lifetime_desktop.h. Embedders must migrate to the newer ProfileBrowserCollection and GlobalBrowserCollection APIs and update include paths. (1d867aa) (e9e4c74)
    BrowserUIProfiles
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public API method WebContentsDelegate::OnDidBlockNavigation has a new signature. Embedders overriding this method must update their implementation to include the new initiator_url and initiator_origin parameters, which provide more context on the source of a blocked navigation. (64a041d)
    BrowserNavigation
  • ⚠️BREAKING: On macOS, core application classes BrowserCrApplication and AppShimApplication were refactored to inherit from a new CrApplication base class. This change in the class hierarchy is a breaking change for embedders on macOS who subclass or directly interact with these application delegates. (168b5dd)
    macOSBrowser
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The core MojomParse trait in Rust Mojo bindings has been modified to include a Context parameter. This breaks the previous stateless design and requires manual updates for all custom serialization/deserialization logic in projects using Rust Mojo. (bf955d0)
    BaseInfra
  • The SecurityPrincipal interface now includes a new GetHost() method to directly access the host portion of a site's URL, simplifying code that previously had to parse it from the full site URL. (dbde8fb)
    BaseSecurity
  • Significant work was done on the base/i18n library to improve language code handling, including the introduction of a RegionCode class and optimizations to avoid string allocations. (c21b288) (bb786c6)
    BaseI18n

Sync

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The deprecated methods IsSyncFeatureEnabled() and IsSyncFeatureActive() have been removed from the public SyncService API. Callers must now use GetUserSettings()->GetSelectedOsTypes() or GetActiveDataTypes(). (9efc428)
    Sync
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The SyncPrefs public API for handling encryption bootstrap tokens has changed. The GetEncryptionBootstrapTokenForAccount and SetEncryptionBootstrapTokenForAccount methods now use the new CustomPassphraseBootstrapToken class instead of a raw std::string. (11382d9)
    SyncCrypto
  • A crash caused by corrupted local Nigori (sync encryption) data has been fixed. The system now validates the data on startup and can safely reset it if invalid, rather than crashing the browser. (0ab182f)
    SyncCrypto

Extensions

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The ExtensionKeybindingRegistry::Delegate class has been removed. The constructor for ExtensionKeybindingRegistry now requires a TabListInterface* instead of the delegate, abstracting away platform differences for getting the active tab. (f08221c)
    ExtensionsUI
  • A Time-of-Check Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) race condition in the speech recognition API that allowed extensions to bypass audio capture permissions has been fixed. (0086720)
    ExtensionsSecurityMedia
  • The ability for extensions to move Isolated Web Apps (IWAs) into a tabbed browser via chrome.tabs.move or chrome.tabs.group has been blocked to enforce security boundaries. (47d8324)
    ExtensionsSecurityTabs

Security & Privacy

  • A fix in the Password Manager now correctly checks the entire ancestor chain of an iframe for cross-origin contexts, preventing cleartext passwords from being sent to a renderer process in certain "sandwich" iframe scenarios. (b80505d)
    PasswordManagerSecurity
  • An Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR) bypass vulnerability was fixed in the audio service. Raw this pointers are no longer logged, replaced by unguessable tokens. (552949a)
    SecurityMedia
  • The browser is now protected against crashes from invalid URLs written to the clipboard. ScopedClipboardWriter::WriteURL will now silently drop invalid URLs instead of causing a crash in DCHECK builds. (ca8919b)
    UISecurity
  • Printing requests originating from Fenced Frames are now rejected to prevent potential exploits. (07e3684)
    SecurityPrinting

Build & Dependencies

  • ⚠️BREAKING: A dependency roll for Harfbuzz has bumped the minimum required version of FreeType to 2.8.0. This will break builds for embedders who provide their own, older version of the FreeType library. (8c34f88)
    BuildFontsInfra
  • The Rust toolchain was repackaged to reflect a change in its version reporting, with no change to the underlying compiler version. (748a3f2)
    BuildInfra

Enterprise

  • The AppLaunchAutomation enterprise policy and its associated handler code have been removed. While the feature was from M117, its complete removal will break any administrative configurations that still reference this policy. (94ea7ec)
    EnterprisePolicy

UI & UX

  • A bug was fixed where detaching and re-attaching a tab would cause its window to lose its original size and position. (ef121df)
    UITabs
  • A bug where holding the Esc key would not exit fullscreen mode if focus was on a native UI element like the Omnibox has been fixed. (92368d9)
    UIBrowser
  • Several unnecessary items (emoji, writing direction, look up) were removed from the Omnibox context menu as part of a UI refresh. (e9141f4)
    UIBrowser

2026-05-17

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-17

Overview

This digest analyzes 7 commits from the Chromium repository. After filtering for changes relevant to Windows and macOS desktop platforms and excluding internal test refactoring, 3 distinct changes were identified. No major breaking changes were found in this set of commits. The focus of this batch was on UI text updates, test infrastructure improvements, and routine translation updates.

Browser UI

  • Updated UI strings to correctly display pluralization for the 'Sharing n tabs' counter in contextual tasks and the compose box. (3f982b3)
    UII18n

Testing

  • Several unit tests, including SyncUIUtilTestWithBrowser and signin_util_unittest.cc, were refactored to use lighter-weight test fixtures. This removes dependencies on BrowserWithTestWindowTest, improving test efficiency without affecting browser functionality. (d018942) (6ca077d)
    TestingSync
  • The internal WaitTool, used for automation, now enforces a URL check on tabs to improve the reliability of automated test suites. (5b8b5c3)
    TestingBrowser

Internationalization

  • A routine update was performed on translation string files (.xtb) to synchronize them with the latest changes from the source .grd files. (ca327f2)
    I18n

2026-05-16

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-16

Overview

This digest covers 55 commits from the Chromium repository. After filtering based on relevance to browser embedders on desktop platforms (Windows/macOS) and ignoring internal web platform, OS-specific, and minor changes, this report focuses on key updates in GPU, Security, UI, and PDF functionalities. One major breaking change was identified in the GPU control list API, alongside several important security hardenings for device interactions and extensions.

GPU

  • ⚠️The GpuControlList::MakeDecision method signature has been changed, removing the default value for the ignored_entries parameter. Callers must now explicitly provide a std::vector<uint32_t>, which may require updates for embedders using this GPU blocklisting interface. (c7427fc)
    GPUAPI
  • The helper function IOSurfaceIsWebGPUCompatible() has been moved from //media/base/mac to //ui/gfx/mac to resolve a dependency conflict where //gpu could not depend on //media. This is a code organization refactor primarily affecting macOS builds that interact with WebGPU and IOSurfaces. (59ad18d)
    GPUmacOSMedia

Security

  • The WebUSB implementation on macOS has been hardened against a potential Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerability. The fix prevents claiming a USB interface if it advertises an endpoint address already claimed by another interface, mitigating risks from maliciously crafted USB devices. (a74fd42)
    USBSecuritymacOS
  • A potential shortcut hijacking vulnerability in the extensions' Global Shortcuts portal has been fixed. The change ensures that suggested accelerators for manifest-defined commands are cleared if they are not globally active, preventing malicious extensions from suggesting hijacks of reserved browser keys. (39567c3)
    ExtensionsSecurity
  • A bounds check was added to the HID connection logic on Windows to prevent a potential buffer over-read. This hardens the driver against devices that could cause bytes_transferred to exceed the buffer size after internal processing. (0bac4a7)
    SecurityWindows

UI

  • Native UI rendering via gfx::RenderText has been improved to correctly display text-default emoji codepoints followed by a variation selector (VS-16) in color. This fixes an issue on Windows and macOS where characters like '©️' or '™️' would render as monochrome glyphs in tab titles and other native UI surfaces, aligning their appearance with web content. (1c45c78)
    UIFontsmacOS

PDF

  • The internal implementation for PDF Ink Signatures continues to be built out. Recent changes add backend logic for managing text annotations, including showing, editing, deleting, and handling undo/redo states. This work is part of an ongoing feature development within the PDF viewer. (f05ce42) (b1809ed) (04cd755) (4f6437d) (feed479) (f880d93) (f469ee2)
    PDFUI

Web Apps

  • The Web App launch process has been refactored to use the central browser_navigator, consolidating navigation logic. Additionally, the chrome://web-app-internals debug page's JSON structure was normalized from an array to a dictionary, improving frontend type safety and maintainability. (fe7265c) (f058bd2)
    BrowserUI

2026-05-15

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-15

Overview

On May 15, 2026, the Chromium main branch saw 729 commits, with 555 being relevant to developers building on the platform. This day was marked by a significant number of breaking API changes across the UI, Autofill, Networking, and Media stacks as part of ongoing modernization efforts. Notable updates also landed in the Extensions framework, security hardening, and performance optimizations for GPU process initialization. The first relevant commit was (5dae0b1) and the last was (f98f0f4).

Breaking API Changes

  • ⚠️The widely used views::Textfield control has been updated to use ui::ColorId instead of SkColor. The public methods SetPlaceholderTextColor(), set_text_color(), and set_selection_text_color() have been removed and replaced by their *_id suffixed counterparts (e.g., set_text_color_id()). (32b7f60) (f262b03) (f20aaa7)
    UI
  • ⚠️The public virtual method AutofillManager::OnFormsSeen has changed its signature to take vector arguments by value for move semantics, instead of by const reference. Subclasses overriding this method must update their signature. (97de7f3)
    Autofill
  • ⚠️The public method PaymentsDataManager::SetCreditCards has been removed. Embedders must now use more specific functions like AddCreditCard to manage local credit card data. (4f609cf)
    PaymentsAutofill
  • ⚠️The public method Browser::IsPointerLocked() has been removed. Callers must now access this state through the ExclusiveAccessManager. (760506d)
    BrowserUI
  • ⚠️A large-scale rename occurred, changing "borderless" to "unframed" for app window modes. Public APIs like BrowserView::AppUsesBorderlessMode() are now AppUsesUnframedMode(). (5eb2a98)
    UIBrowser
  • ⚠️The public class content::PrefetchUpdateHeadersParams has been moved and renamed to network::HttpRequestHeadersUpdateParams, breaking embedders using the class from its previous content location. (a89eecd)
    NetworkPerformance
  • ⚠️The audio::SoundsManager singleton has been refactored. The static Create, Shutdown, and Get methods have been moved to a new audio::GlobalSoundsManager class, changing how system sounds are accessed. (c253096)
    MediaUI
  • ⚠️The WebstoreInstallHelper::Delegate::InstallHelperResultCode enum was converted to an enum class, requiring implementors of this public delegate to update to scoped enum names (e.g., InstallHelperResultCode::kIconError). (8ea05c8)
    Extensions
  • ⚠️In the public device_management_backend.proto, the EXTENSION_UPDATE_CHECK remote command type has been renamed to BROWSER_EXTENSION_UPDATE_CHECK, affecting enterprise management clients. (ce1de7f)
    EnterprisePolicy
  • ⚠️The content_bounds member has been removed from the public viz::OutputSurfaceFrame struct, breaking custom viz::OutputSurface implementations. (bf710c2)
    CompositorGPU
  • ⚠️The target_output_channel_layout_ member of media::AudioDecoderConfig has been changed from ChannelLayout to ChannelLayoutConfig, breaking code that constructs or inspects this media structure. (9a0e8b3)
    Media
  • ⚠️The mechanism for selecting a GPU for XR has changed. The --xr-device-adapter-luid command-line switch is no longer the primary method, and control has moved to GpuDataManager. (62dc843)
    XRGPU
  • ⚠️The public API for download bubble components now uses BrowserWindowInterface* instead of Browser*, affecting the DownloadDisplayController constructor and related utilities. (371912c)
    DownloadsUI
  • ⚠️The TopContainerLoadingBar class and its associated APIs have been removed from BrowserView and BrowserViewLayout, breaking any code referencing these defunct UI components. (485812b)
    UIBrowser
  • ⚠️The public class FrameworkSpecificImplementation in ui/base/class_factory.h has been renamed to BaseImplementation. Embedders using this class for platform-specific UI must update to the new name. (727bd8f)
    UIBase

UI & Browser

  • A new system for handling icons in WebUI toolbars was introduced, using an IconTable and IconHandle to manage icons more efficiently than passing data URLs. (17201e8) (b3f0916)
    UI
  • The ImmersiveModeController is now initialized earlier in the browser creation lifecycle, decoupling it from the BrowserWindow and simplifying dependencies. (8138d14) (f7767ae)
    UIBrowser
  • The WidgetFadeAnimator was updated to support an optional sliding animation in addition to fading. (30d4a03)
    UI
  • On Windows, a bug was fixed where mouse clicks in the non-client area (e.g., title bar) were not handled correctly while a menu was open, preventing incorrect UI behavior. (7432702)
    UIWindows

Extensions

  • A new API has been added to persist MIME handler options. The setMimeHandlerOptions and getMimeHandlerOptions functions allow extensions to manage which MIME types they handle. (5f78f2f)
    Extensions
  • Security was tightened by enforcing the documented behavior that manifest-sandboxed extension pages cannot open message channels. (885bc09)
    ExtensionsSecurity
  • A bug was fixed where storage.onChanged events triggered by non-extension sources (like Chrome Sync) would incorrectly leak to untrusted content scripts. The event now correctly respects the storage area's access level. (19a5003)
    ExtensionsStorageSecurity
  • The reliability of service worker registration was improved by fixing race conditions and ensuring preferences are cleared correctly on unregistration. (bd9a81f) (66fe3ad)
    ExtensionsServiceWorker
  • A bug in the update service was fixed that prevented pending extensions synced from a user's account from being installed. (204edb6)
    ExtensionsSync

Security & Privacy

  • The Web Bluetooth API was updated to correctly check the GATT blocklist for a descriptor's parent characteristic, preventing websites from bypassing security restrictions. (a0b55d7)
    BluetoothSecurity
  • The WebUSB API now rejects forbidden Standard control transfers, such as SET_CONFIGURATION, which must be performed via dedicated WebIDL methods. (3169e98)
    USBSecurity
  • A potential SQL injection vulnerability in the AutocompleteTable was fixed by properly escaping user input before database queries. (7a863df)
    PasswordManagerSecurity
  • To prevent potential undefined behavior, a virtual destructor was added to the autofill::WebauthnDialog abstract base class. (70b7db2)
    AutofillSecurity

Networking & Storage

  • Startup performance is improved by sending a not-yet-connected GPU channel pipe to the renderer during initialization. This allows the renderer to establish its GPU channel more quickly without a full roundtrip to the browser. (d98234c)
    NetworkGPUPerformance
  • The InactivityTimer class has been moved from content/browser/indexed_db to the more general components/services/storage/public/cpp to allow its use by other storage components. (c0811f6)
    StorageBase

Infrastructure & Testing

  • To aid in testing, an optional parameter was added to GpuControlList::MakeDecision to allow ignoring specific blocklist entries. (fa2e72d)
    GPUTesting
  • A recent change to base/rand_util.h was reverted to fix a startup crash on Windows. (72339f3)
    BaseInfraWindows
  • To prevent build conflicts, generated code for Protobuf's Well Known Types (e.g., any.proto) has been removed from the source tree. (3822f95)
    BuildInfra

2026-05-14

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-14

Overview

This summary covers 445 relevant commits out of 597 total for the main branch on May 14th, 2026. This digest focuses on changes impacting developers working on Chromium-based projects, with a special emphasis on breaking changes, APIs, security, and browser-level features for Windows and macOS. The day's activity started with commit (5e8bd89) and concluded with (3eec1c7).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The variations::VariationsServiceClient interface has been modified. The methods RemoveGoogleGroupsFromPrefsForDeletedProfiles and RemoveEnterpriseGroupsFromPrefsForDeletedProfiles have been removed, and embedders must now implement the new pure virtual method GetAllProfilesKeys. (07c8aa0)
    APIBrowserEnterprise
  • ⚠️The virtual method URLLoaderThrottle::BeforeWillRedirectRequest signature has changed. It now takes a const net::RedirectInfo& instead of a pointer, and parameters for modifying request headers have been removed. Overriding classes must be updated. (8c41713)
    NetworkAPI
  • ⚠️The mojo interfaces for Service Workers have been updated to prevent a race condition. blink::mojom::EmbeddedWorkerInstanceHost::RequestTermination and blink::mojom::ServiceWorker::AddKeepAlive now require a uint64_t sequence number, breaking integrations that use these interfaces. (d92cc43)
    ServiceWorkerAPI
  • ⚠️The remoting component has been cleaned up. The public classes JitterBuffer and InMemoryFifoBuffer have been deleted. Additionally, the signature of AudioInjector::SetSampleInfo has changed to take a base::OnceCallback<void(bool success)>, requiring updates for custom audio injection implementations. (6ab855d)
    APIMedia
  • ⚠️The ui/views dialog API has been modified. DialogDelegate::set_frame_margins now accepts a FrameMarginsParams struct instead of FrameMargins. Embedders creating custom dialogs must update their code. (651cf21)
    UIAPI
  • ⚠️A new pure virtual method, GetExpectedDisplayTime(), has been added to the public blink::WebVideoFrameSubmitter interface. Embedders with custom implementations must provide this method. (94af377)
    MediaAPI
  • ⚠️The cc::ImageAnimationController::Client nested class has been renamed to cc::ImageAnimationController::Delegate. Implementations of this interface must be updated. (4a9f742)
    CompositorAPI
  • ⚠️In the cc::BrowserControlsOffsetManagerClient interface, the RenderedDeviceScaleFactor() method has been renamed to DeviceScaleFactor(). Implementations must be updated. (c923203)
    CompositorUIAPI
  • ⚠️The BackgroundTracingManager has been moved from content/ to services/tracing/. Embedders using this system must update include paths and call the new factory function content::CreateBackgroundTracingManager. (0be4281)
    APIPerformance
  • ⚠️The build visibility of the third-party XML parser, //third_party/expat:expat, has been restricted. Downstream projects with a direct dependency may see build failures and need to be added to an allowlist. (4eaa1b7)
    Build
  • ⚠️Headers for the PartitionAlloc memory allocator have been split into public and internal sets. This may break builds for embedders who were including headers now considered internal. (7c766eb)
    BaseBuild
  • ⚠️The device::mojom::SensorProvider::GetSensor method signature has changed to include a new SensorConnectionWatcher remote. Clients calling or implementing this mojom interface will need to be updated. (19452b0)
    APISecurity

APIs & Core Interfaces

  • A new aura::Window::GetWeakPtrAsWindow() method was added to simplify lifetime management and encourage using base::WeakPtr over WindowTracker for single windows, helping prevent use-after-free bugs. (d81edbd)
    UIBase
  • A new WindowFeatureController class has been introduced to centralize checks for which window features a Browser instance supports, as part of a larger refactoring effort. (9527174)
    BrowserUI
  • The experimental DeclarativePerformanceObserver feature, which allows websites to record telemetry from navigation start, continues development with the addition of an HTTP header parser and a skeleton PageLoadMetricsObserver. (2d4c09d) (de01842)
    PerformanceAPI
  • The sql::Database transaction methods have been hardened to better handle cases where the database is closed during a transaction, preventing crashes and improving testability. (1ee70dc) (9574a48)
    StorageBase
  • A virtual destructor has been added to MessageMapInterface in ui/gfx/win/window_impl.h to prevent potential resource leaks on Windows when custom implementations are used. (83e1b6f)
    UIWindows

Security

  • To mitigate a Site Isolation bypass, validation for MHTML subframes has been tightened to enforce that they commit with opaque origins, preventing a compromised renderer from spoofing its origin. (e4b92a6)
    SecurityNavigation
  • Missing UUID allowlist checks were added to bluetooth::Adapter's RegisterAdvertisement and CreateLocalGattService methods, preventing compromised sandboxed processes from spoofing BLE advertisements or GATT services. (08e384b)
    BluetoothSecurity
  • The browser will now terminate a renderer process if it supplies invalid keys to IndexedDB, hardening the browser against compromised renderers. (206286e)
    StorageSecurity
  • The Chromium updater has been hardened by improving temporary directory selection and sanitizing manifest paths to block directory traversal attacks. (58d2139)
    UpdaterSecurity
  • The Incognito mode URL blocklist policy implementation was refactored and moved to a more central location at components/policy/core/browser/url_list/. (c9cc012)
    PolicySecurityPrivacy

Browser & UI

  • A new feature allows users to drag a URL or file onto the toolbar area (excluding the omnibox) to open it in the current tab, improving user workflow. (2ce75bc)
    UIBrowser
  • A new surface synchronization mechanism has been introduced for WebUI to align the initial rendering of embedded toolbars with the top-level browser window, controlled by the kBypassOutdatedSurfaceActivation feature flag. (98d27c8)
    UICompositor
  • On macOS, the browser frame's glass effect will now use NSGlassEffectView on newer OS versions to better align with platform UX designs. (06e2d61)
    UImacOS
  • A potential race condition during browser shutdown on macOS has been fixed by ensuring async profile-load callbacks correctly handle cases where a profile is no longer available. (3a9ac96)
    BrowsermacOSProfiles
  • A toast notification will now be displayed to confirm when Chrome has been successfully set as the default browser. (2bdf836)
    UIBrowser

Extensions

  • A recent change that broke PDF printing from extensions has been reverted. The SetUpBeforeUnloadControl() function was found to be necessary for this functionality. (e0194c7)
    ExtensionsPrinting
  • The behavior of the search engine override dialog is now configurable via a Finch flag, allowing for testing of a less intrusive version that appears only once per session. (63bc242)
    ExtensionsBrowser
  • The main extensions menu will now automatically close when a context menu is opened from one of its items, improving UI consistency. (9979b04)
    ExtensionsUI

Build & Testing

  • Several components, including chrome_xr_integration_client and browser_activity_watcher, were refactored to have header-only source_set targets. This allows downstream targets to include their headers without depending on the large //chrome/browser:browser monolith. (aebb18d) (aee6563) (066fd15)
    BuildBrowser
  • Test-only functions like SetAutoAcceptWebAppDialogForTesting have been replaced with modern, RAII-style scoped helpers (ScopedAutoAcceptWebAppDialogs) to prevent state leakage between tests. (43fb711)
    TestingBrowser
  • The build toolchain detection logic was updated to recognize the "Insiders" preview channel for Visual Studio 2026. (1550583)
    BuildWindows
  • The build process for Protobuf's well-known-types is being updated to prefer generating code at build time, helping resolve conflicts between "full" and "lite" protobuf variants. (0360593)
    BuildInfra

2026-05-13

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-13

Overview

On 2026-05-13, the Chromium main branch saw 682 commits, with 499 identified as relevant to developers. This digest focuses on changes impacting APIs, extensions, security, and browser internals for Windows and macOS. Key events include several breaking API changes in memory management, extensions, and graphics. The first and last relevant commits were (5b6de9c) and (010fba6).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The chrome.webviewTag API and the underlying <webview> tag functionality have been removed from Chrome Apps on Windows, macOS, and Linux. This is part of the ongoing deprecation of Chrome Apps and removes a major, stable feature. (dfa28c4)
    ExtensionsAPI
  • ⚠️A recently added feature allowing extensions (via webRequest and declarativeNetRequest APIs) to modify the User-Agent request header has been reverted and removed. This is a significant behavioral change that breaks extensions relying on this capability. (079b3a3)
    ExtensionsNetworkAPI
  • ⚠️Several core PartitionAlloc headers (e.g., partition_root.h, thread_cache.h) have been moved into an internal/ subdirectory. This is a breaking change for embedders who directly include these headers for deep integration with Chromium's memory allocator, as it modifies the public API surface of //base/allocator. (cd836a8)
    BaseBuild
  • ⚠️Core graphics API signatures, including gpu::RasterImplementation::MapRasterCHROMIUM and cc::PaintOp::Serialize, have been updated to use base::span instead of raw pointers and size arguments. Embedders using these low-level graphics interfaces will need to update their code. (8235863)
    GPUCompositorAPI
  • ⚠️The public API of feature_engagement::Tracker has been restricted. Methods like ShouldTriggerHelpUI and Dismissed are now protected. Client code must migrate to the new NonIphPromo helper class for querying the tracker for non-IPH promotional UI. (8fdc996)
    UIAPI
  • ⚠️The public remoting::AudioInjector class has been refactored. It no longer inherits from protocol::AudioStub, and the InjectAudioPacket() method has been removed in favor of a new SetSampleInfo() method. Embedders using this for custom audio injection must adapt to the new API. (5259bac)
    MediaNetworkAPI
  • ⚠️The public header extensions/browser/media_capture_util.h and its utility functions (GrantMediaStreamRequest, VerifyMediaAccessPermission) have been deleted. Embedders who were using this utility must find an alternative. (e2a0758)
    ExtensionsMediaAPI
  • ⚠️The pure virtual method OnSendTabToSelfModelLoaded() has been removed from the public send_tab_to_self::SendTabToSelfModelObserver interface. Any class implementing this observer will fail to compile and must remove the method override. (2ca3334)
    SyncAPI
  • ⚠️In the experimental Email Verification Protocol, the nonce attribute has been renamed to challenge. This changes the public FormFieldData struct, renaming the nonce() method to challenge(). Embedders using Autofill components must update their code. (0846112)
    AutofillAPI

APIs & Extensions

  • The Fetch and XMLHttpRequest APIs can now set a custom User-Agent header, controlled by the new kUserAgentFollowingSpec feature flag. This aligns browser behavior with web specifications. (c7d5f1c)
    NetworkAPI
  • The delivery mechanism for the Autofill Email Verification Protocol (EVP) token has changed. Instead of a DOM event, the token is now delivered by setting the value of an input field with autocomplete="email-verification-token" upon form submission. This involved renaming DispatchEmailVerifiedEvent to SendEmailVerificationToken in the mojom::AutofillAgent interface. (3a04bbe) (b829b41)
    AutofillAPI
  • The Manifest V2 deprecation checker will now disable extensions of type TYPE_USER_SCRIPT, treating them the same as regular extensions to ensure they are phased out. (72af005)
    Extensions
  • The UnexportableKeyService interface was updated with stub methods for key generation and a new UnexportableAttestationKeyId type, in preparation for supporting Device Bound Session Credentials. (89c81c4) (39bf1e8)
    SecurityAPICrypto

Security

  • The network sandbox on Windows now brokers connections to all non-publicly-routable IP addresses. This routes local traffic through the unsandboxed browser process, fixing ERR_NETWORK_ACCESS_DENIED errors for users on certain VPN or virtualized network setups. (3129fd2)
    SecurityNetworkWindows
  • A vulnerability allowing a compromised MediaFoundation CDM process to bypass site isolation on Windows has been fixed. The process is no longer granted broad read access to the root CDM storage directory, preventing it from accessing the DRM state of other origins. (78e86d3)
    SecurityMediaWindows
  • The Web Serial API now blocks access for HTTP/HTTPS origins running in a non-default StoragePartition. This prevents guest views from persisting permissions that could leak to the main profile. (16e3c60)
    SecurityAPIStorage
  • To mitigate cross-protocol attacks, the HttpStreamPool now validates that ports advertised by Alt-Svc are not on the restricted list (e.g., port 25 for SMTP) before connecting. (f40c463)
    SecurityNetwork

Performance & Core Internals

  • navigator.clipboard.read() and readText() now use asynchronous Mojo IPCs. This prevents the renderer main thread from freezing while waiting for the OS to provide clipboard data, especially during delayed rendering scenarios. (a274f36)
    PerformanceAPIUI
  • A bug was fixed on macOS where GPU backpressure fences were not being forwarded correctly, causing the GPU to produce frames without synchronization and leading to system-wide UI lag. This restores correct GPU backpressure. (bc2f1fe)
    PerformanceGPUmacOS
  • The PartitionAllocWithAdvancedChecks (MiracleObject) feature has been enabled by default for the Browser UI thread to enhance memory safety and stability. (34c2ad4)
    PerformanceBaseSecurity
  • The base::SpanReader utility is now constexpr and includes new methods that return std::optional, making it more ergonomic for parsing data from spans at compile time and runtime. (2a0da15)
    Base

Browser UI & Features

  • The underlying logic for restoring a "split tab" (a window with two tabs side-by-side) has been implemented behind the kSplitViewTabRestore flag. This handles restoring normal, pinned, and grouped split tabs, though session restore persistence is not yet complete. (c25e0e5)
    UITabsBrowser
  • A new infobar has been added on macOS to alert users when a Keychain issue prevents Chromium from reading or writing encrypted data like cookies and passwords. (90b9499)
    UImacOSSecurity
  • The Fluent scrollbar on Windows has been updated to correctly change the color of its arrows when hovered, matching the behavior of the thumb for better visual consistency. (39ce5ba)
    UIWindows
  • The management of ui::Layer property requests has been refactored into a consolidated, RAII-based architecture using a new ScopedLayerRequest template. This simplifies the compositor API and improves safety by ensuring automatic cleanup. (92fc309) (234f30b)
    UICompositor

Testing & Infrastructure

  • The services and controllers for the desktop download bubble have been extracted from the main chrome/browser target into a dedicated component (//chrome/browser/download/bubble/), improving modularity and build times. (5cb5cdf)
    DownloadsUIBuild
  • Several sync-related unit tests have been refactored to use the more capable FakeOpenTabsUIDelegate instead of mocks, enabling more robust and stateful testing of tab sync features. (b37b6e2)
    TestingSync
  • The test launcher will now log an explicit and clear error message when a test suite times out, making it easier for developers to diagnose CI failures. (9ff496a)
    TestingInfra

2026-05-12

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-12

Overview

This summary covers 551 relevant commits out of 729 total for the main branch on May 12, 2026. The changes span from the first commit (fb302b7a27a1ca09aa27e063d93207d34d7a6d53) to the last (4f918219e10f678a63b41100a410177ca768284e), featuring a significant number of breaking API changes across networking, autofill, UI, and core services that require developer attention.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️Multiple core networking interfaces have been changed. A crs_root_id field was added to net::CertVerifyResult and net::SSLInfo. The net::ConnectionChangeNotifier::Observer::OnSessionClosed signature was updated with a new boolean parameter. Finally, mojom::GeolocationContext::BindGeolocation now requires a const url::Origin& instead of a GURL. Embedders using or implementing these will need to update their code. (e41c4fa) (7034e9a) (40d7259)
    NetworkSecuritySSL
  • ⚠️The Autofill component introduced new pure virtual methods to its client interfaces. ShowOmniboxAutofillChip() was added to autofill::OmniboxAutofillDelegate and HideOmniboxAutofillChip() was added to autofill::PaymentsAutofillClient. Custom implementations of these interfaces must be updated. (e81f974) (240d9ee)
    AutofillUIBrowser
  • ⚠️Several public APIs and services have been removed. The AccessibilityAnnotatorService and EntityDataProvider interface for Autofill AI are gone. The AuthenticatorListObserver and ObservableAuthenticatorList for WebAuthn have been removed. The deprecated RequestType::kLocalNetworkAccess has also been fully removed. (96558fe) (68f4910) (58ff1a8)
    AutofillSecurityBrowser
  • ⚠️The public function chrome::NewSplitTab now requires a new layout parameter of type split_tabs::SplitTabLayout. Callers must be updated to specify the desired split layout (e.g., horizontal or vertical). (72e8f4b)
    BrowserUITabs
  • ⚠️The signature for PluginUtils::GetExtensionIdForMimeType has been changed to include a new bool embedded parameter. Callers must now specify whether the load is for an embedded context. (edaf302)
    ExtensionsBrowser
  • ⚠️On macOS, the virtual method BeginFrameSource::UpdateVSyncDisplay() now requires a display_id parameter. Embedders with custom BeginFrameSource implementations will need to update their code. (7d3e638)
    macOSCompositorGPU
  • ⚠️The compositor (cc) library changed the cc::PropertyTree::Node() method signature from returning a pointer to a const reference, and added a new MutableNode() method. This requires updating all call sites to ensure non-null access and const correctness. (56af65f)
    CompositorGPU
  • ⚠️The ReadAnythingController::OnEntrypointShown method signature was changed by removing the default value for its user_initiated parameter, requiring all callers to explicitly provide the argument. (a4d3bc9)
    UIAccessibilityBrowser
  • ⚠️The kBlockV8OptimizerOnUnfamiliarSites feature flag was removed, making its behavior permanent. Code that programmatically enabled or disabled this feature will no longer compile. (ff37e94)
    PerformanceSecurity

Security & Privacy

  • Service Workers can now intercept download requests initiated from browser UI actions like "Save As". This aligns behavior with web standards but may be a significant behavioral change for embedders who relied on UI-initiated downloads bypassing Service Workers. (a2fbe85)
    ServiceWorkerDownloadsSecurity
  • For improved security, ChromeDriver now enforces IP address restriction checks for WebSocket connections in addition to standard HTTP requests. (a47a1ba)
    TestingSecurityDevTools
  • Screenshot redaction for sensitive fields in Out-of-Process Iframes (OOPIFs) has been hardened. The browser now ensures all frames are processed for redaction, preventing a compromised frame from bypassing privacy measures. (f588883)
    PrivacySecurityUI
  • The ChildProcessSecurityPolicy has been refactored to use a consolidated Rust HashMap for known schemes, improving type safety and removing runtime checks. (d99f709)
    SecurityBase

Extensions & APIs

  • The extension API binding system was refactored, changing how asynchronous function signatures are handled for Promise-based APIs. The system now ignores whether a callback is marked as optional in the schema. This is a significant behavioral change that could affect extensions relying on previous signature validation. (5498842)
    ExtensionsBase
  • The alarms.create API has been enhanced with a new optional persistAcrossSessions parameter, which defaults to true to maintain existing behavior. (900c2b0)
    Extensions
  • The stream pipeline for MIME handlers now caches the response body. This allows extensions to use chrome.mimeHandler.abortAndFallbackToNativeHandler without forcing a re-download of the content. (f4ddf8e)
    ExtensionsDownloadsNetwork

Browser, UI & Services

  • A new cpu_performance API and user setting have been added to allow overriding the performance tier reported to websites via navigator.cpuPerformance. This also exposes new functions via the public content API to get CPU model info. (2e3a831)
    PerformancePrivacyBrowser
  • A new OmniboxAutofillPageActionController has been implemented to manage an "Autofill payment" chip in the omnibox, introducing new UI elements and logic. (1c654fa)
    AutofillUIBrowser
  • On macOS, the updater can now read Omaha 4 tags that are "wedged" into .pkg installer files. This allows for better installation tracking without breaking package signing. (efea721)
    UpdatermacOS
  • A potential use-after-free crash in ui/views/controls/menu/menu_controller.cc was fixed by adding a validity check after mouse release events. (80bd90c)
    UIBase
  • Support was added for successful HTTP/3 WebSocket handshakes, which use a 200 response code rather than the 101 response used by HTTP/1.1. (d8afde9)
    NetworkWebRTC

Testing & Infrastructure

  • Traffic annotations are now disallowed in C++ header files (.h) to ensure they are defined in implementation files for better tracking and to prevent cyclic dependencies. (33bd18c)
    NetworkBuildPrivacy
  • A new test-only builder was introduced for DeviceInfo to simplify the creation of test objects and improve test readability in sync-related unit tests. (3160818)
    TestingSync
  • The third-party libraries libpng and SQLite were updated to versions 1.6.58 and 3.53.1, respectively. (76f7f05) (163b530)
    InfraBuild

2026-05-11

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-11

Overview

This summary covers 456 relevant commits out of 596 total for the main branch on May 11, 2026. The changes span from the first commit b458747572a958bad3620a6b0e58590f0013313d to the last 6ab45a706ac3b7648d843a64a8c9d505e7439348. This digest focuses on breaking changes, new APIs, and updates to networking, security, and the browser UI relevant to developers building on Chromium.

APIs and Services

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The content::ContentBrowserClient and content::WebContentsObserver interfaces have been updated with new pure virtual methods (GetWebContentsFromNativeWindow, OnChildWebContentsAttached, OnChildWebContentsDetached). Embedders subclassing these interfaces must provide implementations to avoid compilation errors. (3221e4a) (4a7f0a4)
    APIsBrowser
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The content::DesktopMediaID struct, used for screen and window capture, has been modified. Its string serialization format has changed (e.g., to window:1:2:s), which will break code that parses this string. A new public API, content::desktop_capture::GetApplicationAudioCaptureId, has also been added for macOS. (045d84e)
    APIsMediamacOS
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The WebShare API is now blocked from being triggered by inactive WebContents. This is a significant behavioral change that will break applications relying on background tabs initiating a share action. (38cdd66)
    APIsBrowser
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The signature of ContentSettingsProvider::SetWebsiteSetting has been changed from taking a base::Value&& to a const base::Value&. Custom content setting providers must be updated. (41de479)
    APIsBrowserStorage
  • ⚠️BREAKING: On Windows, audio devices routed through hardware DSPs will now have a (Bluetooth) suffix appended if the underlying device is Bluetooth. This can break applications that rely on exact string matching for device names. (656c5ba)
    WindowsMediaAPIs
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The device::mojom::XRPresentationProvider::SubmitFrameDrawnIntoTexture mojom interface signature has been changed to include a new camera_sync_tokens parameter, affecting the public WebXR API. (02a16b8)
    XRAPIs
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The AccessibilityAnnotatorEnablementServiceFactory has been removed and its functionality migrated to PersonalContextEnablementServiceFactory in a new component. Consumers of the old factory will need to update their code and dependencies. (30d3203)
    BrowserServices
  • A new function, GetInitialBrowserCommandLine(), has been added to chrome/app/chrome_main.h, providing a read-only snapshot of the command line at startup before it is modified by internal processes. (cd36976)
    BaseBrowser

Networking

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The network::mojom::RestrictedCookieManager::SetCookieFromString mojom interface signature has been changed. It is now a fire-and-forget method, with a parameter and the CookiesResponse return value removed. (05514ce)
    NetworkStorage
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The net::ConnectJobFactory::NewConnectJob method signature has been changed to accept a network handle to support multi-networking. Custom socket pool implementations must be updated. (6f90916)
    Network
  • ⚠️BREAKING: The public net::HttpResponseInfo::encoded_body_bytes field was changed from std::optional<int64_t> to the unsigned std::optional<base::ByteSize>. Code accessing this field must be updated to handle the new type. (ec45984)
    Network
  • A 'maximum priority' mode has been added for WebSockets, allowing certain internal connections to be created with MAXIMUM_PRIORITY and the LOAD_IGNORE_LIMITS flag. (d09d6a6)
    Network
  • A potential use-after-free crash in the SPDY implementation has been fixed. A synchronous session drain is now deferred to prevent a stream object from being destroyed while in use. (eb721a8)
    NetworkSecurity

Security

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The TargetHandler::Session::Attach method in the DevTools protocol handler now returns a std::optional<std::string> instead of void. This provides better error handling but requires direct users of this content layer API to update their code. (de3302b)
    DevToolsSecurityAPIs
  • A TOCTOU vulnerability in the USB stack was fixed by introducing a lock to reject interface operations while a device configuration change is in progress. (7e97805)
    SecurityUSB
  • A potential Use-After-Unmap vulnerability on Windows serial ports was resolved by keeping Mojo data pipe handles alive until pending I/O completes. (c1ee3a3)
    SecurityWindows
  • A vulnerability allowing a compromised renderer to redirect input to arbitrary cross-origin frames has been patched by validating the FrameSinkId during async hit-testing. (1bdeadd)
    SecurityUI
  • A WebHID policy bypass for GuestViews has been fixed. Guest requests are now always mediated by the embedder via WebViewChooserContext. (63d25d3)
    SecurityExtensionsPolicy
  • The network service now allows authorized renderers, such as extensions with host permissions, to bypass Sec-Fetch-* header restrictions, aligning their capabilities with CORS rules. (55b0dfd)
    SecurityNetworkExtensions

UI and Compositor

  • ⚠️BREAKING: The core compositor interface cc::MutatorHostClient has been renamed to cc::MutatorHostDelegate, including the header file and all related methods (e.g., SetMutatorHostClient). Embedders using this low-level API must update their code. (0394325)
    CompositorUI
  • An issue causing frozen windows after moving Chrome to a different display on macOS has been fixed by improving VSync callback management. (ca74f0e)
    UImacOSCompositor
  • A crash when closing a fullscreen browser on macOS has been fixed. (c96052d)
    UImacOS
  • ResourcePool will now discard all resources when the viewport is resized, significantly reducing memory spikes and improving performance during window resizing. (d6a5cb9)
    PerformanceUI
  • The InfoBarView background color is now set using the kColorInfoBarBackground theme token for better consistency across themes. (116091e)
    UIThemes

Extensions
