  • The long-deprecated and unused app_shell binary and its associated source files have been deleted from the extensions/ directory. (75cd2c6)
    ExtensionsBuild
  • A bug causing guest instance ID collisions in AppView across different profiles has been fixed by including the profile's unique token in the key for pending embed requests. (65c2955)
    ExtensionsProfiles
  • A significant portion of the extensions menu UI code has been migrated from depending on the Browser class to the more abstract BrowserWindowInterface, improving modularity and testability. (d53f97f)
    ExtensionsUI

Build and Code Health

  • The monolithic //chrome/browser:core build target has been modularized into smaller, more focused source_set targets to improve build granularity and parallelism. (a235e83)
    BuildInfra
  • On Windows, the ui/accessibility platform code has been refactored to remove dependencies on ATL, replacing it with WRL and other modern COM implementations. (54afab9)
    BuildWindowsAccessibility
  • A flaky ResourceFetcherTest was fixed by isolating the MemoryCache for each test using ScopedMemoryCacheForTesting, preventing state from leaking between test runs. (b458747)
    TestingInfra

2026-05-10

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-10

Overview

This digest covers 11 commits, focusing on 9 changes relevant to desktop browser UI, graphics, testing, and build systems. Key developments include the default enablement of the 'TreesInViz' feature on Windows and macOS, UI configurability for tab hover effects, and internal refactoring of test utilities. No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified. Changes specific to Android, iOS, ChromeOS, Blink, and V8 were excluded as per filtering criteria.

Graphics & Compositor

  • The TreesInViz feature is now enabled by default on most desktop platforms, including Windows and macOS. This marks a significant internal shift in how layer tree updates are handled by the Viz compositor. A related bug fix ensures screenshot tokens are correctly propagated to prevent timeouts in screenshot tests. (b6929d0) (0099a3c)
    CompositorGPU

Browser UI & Features

  • The delay for the tab glow hover effect is now configurable via a feature parameter, allowing for easier UX experimentation without requiring code changes. (01b3460)
    UITabs
  • Internal refactoring to access TabContextualizationController using the UUD (UserData) pattern, improving code structure. Separately, new metrics were added for the Glic Web UI to track opt-in impressions. (78c3487) (aa9c8cb)
    BrowserUI

Build & Testing

  • A test-only helper function, chrome::IsOffTheRecordBrowserInUse(), has been inlined at its call sites as a preparatory refactor. This change has no impact on production behavior. (f141690)
    TestingBrowser
  • Build system configurations for Protocol Buffers have been improved by adding a dedicated proto_library target for duration.proto, cleaning up dependency management for third-party libraries like nearby. (f3144dd)
    Build
  • CI bot configurations were updated to align with the new default status of the TreesInViz feature across different platforms. (aeba8a1)
    InfraTesting

Internationalization

  • Performed a routine update of translation string files (.xtb) across various components. (128654c)
    I18n

2026-05-09

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-09

Overview

This digest covers 57 commits, with a focus on 9 changes impacting browser APIs, extensions, security, and UI for desktop platforms. Key updates include significant behavioral changes to security enforcement and the Chrome Web Store, a new MIME handler API for extensions, and a breaking refactor of the WebUI user education framework. The changes span from (e8f4b02) to (37bfc56).

Security

  • ⚠️Stricter enforcement of navigation security policies. Renderers that incorrectly provide an Origin header on navigation requests will now be terminated, replacing the previous DumpWithoutCrashing behavior. This is controlled by the kKillOnUnexpectedOriginHeader feature, which is enabled by default. (87ecad0)
    SecurityNetworkBrowser

Extensions

  • ⚠️The component hosted app for the Chrome Web Store will no longer be loaded by default on non-ChromeOS platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux). This changes how navigations to the legacy webstore URL (chrome.google.com/webstore) are handled, treating it as a regular website rather than an app with special privileges. This is controlled by the kWebstoreHostedApp feature. (22c9e8a)
    ExtensionsBrowserSecurity
  • A new API, chrome.mimeHandler.abortAndFallbackToNativeHandler(), has been introduced. This allows third-party MIME handler extensions to surrender a response they cannot render, allowing the browser to fall back to its native handler (e.g., the built-in PDF viewer). (d39ac48)
    ExtensionsAPIBrowser

UI & User Education

  • ⚠️The user education framework for WebUI has been significantly refactored. HelpBubbleHandler is now consolidated with TrackedElementHandler, and the TrackedElementHelpBubbleWebUIAnchor class has been removed. Users of TrackedElementWebUI will see behavioral changes related to element visibility when a WebContents is hidden. (c00f0b9)
    UIAPIBrowser
  • The Omnibox popup architecture is being refactored with the introduction of a new OmniboxPopupFullPresenter class and a new state, OmniboxPopupState::kFull. The LocationBar interface was updated with a new non-pure virtual method, in_popup_state_transition(). (e8f4b02)
    UIBrowser
  • A skeleton for a new client-side API, PersonalContextService, has been added. It introduces the FetchContext method and related data structures for a future Context Memory Service (CMS). (adc0707)
    AIAPIBrowser

Build & Infrastructure

  • The libc++ standard library dependency was rolled forward and then reverted, indicating temporary instability in the build toolchain. A subsequent reland attempts to re-apply the update. (abbeaf7) (ddceba0)
    BuildInfra
  • A new git submodule for a shared public agents repository has been added to //agents/shared, intended to hold shareable agentic configurations. (e754fe8)
    BuildAI

2026-05-08

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-08

Overview

On 2026-05-08, the Chromium main branch saw 677 commits, with 496 being relevant to developers on Windows and macOS. This summary focuses on a significant number of breaking changes to core APIs, along with updates to security, extensions, and the browser UI. The day's activity started with commit (fa57518) and concluded with (d06297d).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public method SiteInstance::GetSiteURL() has been removed as part of a large-scale refactoring. Embedders must now use SiteInstance::GetSecurityPrincipal().GetDeprecatedSiteURL() to access the site URL, which is now owned by the SecurityPrincipal. (01d357d)
    BrowserAPISecurity
  • ⚠️The third-party SQLite library has been rolled from version 3.51.3 to 3.53.1. This is a significant dependency update that may contain C API changes affecting embedders who interact directly with the bundled SQLite. (030b805)
    StorageBaseBuild
  • ⚠️To prevent database corruption, a runtime check has been added that will crash the process if multiple unsynchronized connections are opened to the same sandboxed SQLite database. Embedders must ensure they manage database connections correctly to avoid this crash. (48c4bd9)
    StorageBase
  • ⚠️The signature of WebLocalFrameClient::DidFinishSameDocumentNavigation has been changed to include a new bool caused_by_ad parameter. Embedders implementing this client must update their method signature to match. (30a999b)
    BrowserAPI
  • ⚠️The legacy IPC ParamTraits for ui::LatencyInfo have been removed, along with the entire ui/latency/ipc directory. Embedders still using the legacy latency_info_param_traits.h must migrate to the modern Mojom equivalent. (6942ef8)
    UIInfraAPI
  • ⚠️The virtual Time Now() method has been removed from base::DiscardableSharedMemory and components::discardable_memory::DiscardableSharedMemoryManager. Embedders who were subclassing these and overriding Now() for testing will need to refactor their tests, likely using base::test::TaskEnvironment. (46103bb)
    BaseAPITesting
  • ⚠️The Web Bluetooth API now correctly blocks startNotifications() calls for characteristics on the EXCLUDE_READS blocklist. Applications that were unintentionally relying on this security bypass will no longer function as before. (8b64a2a)
    BluetoothAPISecurity
  • ⚠️Multiple service API signatures have been changed, requiring updates for embedders using them: OneTimeTokenService::Subscribe now requires a source parameter, ActorLoginPermissionCleaningService::ClearConflictingPermissions requires a check_federated_credentials parameter, and SendTabToSelfPageHandler::SendTabToDevice requires an explicit callback. (66bc3eb) (6712934) (3a0f032)
    APISecuritySync
  • ⚠️The public method items() has been removed from the device::HidReportDescriptor class. Embedders using this method to inspect HID report items will need to find an alternative approach. (6328251)
    APIUSB

Security & Privacy

  • A significant use-after-free vulnerability in FileSelectHelper has been fixed by consolidating its lifetime management. The bug could be triggered when asynchronous file operations were interrupted by tab closure. (f2ecc53)
    SecurityUI
  • To prevent UI-spoofing attacks, extension popups are now blocked from appearing when a Digital Credentials API prompt is visible. (dba96c2)
    ExtensionsSecurityUI
  • On macOS, a potential password manager authentication bypass was fixed by ensuring the correct local authentication policy is verified. (6b2a28a)
    SecurityPasswordManagermacOS
  • The Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) persistence feature has been fully launched, and its controlling feature flag, kPersistDeviceBoundSessions, has been removed. (346d24c)
    SecurityNetwork

APIs & Core Services

  • A new method, RenderFrameHost::CouldDisplayBeforeUnloadDialog(), has been added to the public interface, allowing embedders to determine if a frame is in a state that could trigger a "Leave site?" dialog. (8229aec)
    BrowserAPI
  • PickleTraits have been added for url::Origin, allowing this fundamental type to be directly serialized and deserialized in IPC messages. (c6ae625)
    BaseAPI
  • The logic for determining if a browser restart is safe has been improved. It now more accurately detects unsaved form data by requiring both a beforeunload handler and user interaction, reducing false positives. (1e0ea1c)
    BrowserUI
  • A bug in the HTTP/2 implementation has been fixed. A RST_STREAM from the server after a complete response will now correctly return the server's response instead of causing a protocol error. (8bd25dc)
    Network

Extensions

  • The chrome.contextMenus API now supports the "tab" context by default, allowing extensions to add menu items to the tab context menu without requiring an extra permission. (04fa747)
    ExtensionsAPI
  • The File System Access API now allows file pickers to be shown from cross-origin subframes created by MIME handler extensions (e.g., PDF viewers), improving functionality for extensions that render content. (3fd5e2c) (c11cf16)
    ExtensionsAPIStorage
  • A new ExtensionUserActivationService has been introduced to record transient user gestures for extensions, which can be used to grant temporary access to restricted APIs. (aa65205)
    ExtensionsAPI

Browser UI & Accessibility

  • A performance issue causing the browser UI compositor to be unnecessarily throttled has been fixed. This change ensures browser UI animations (e.g., tab dragging) run at the nominal framerate. (5872665)
    PerformanceUICompositor
  • Accessibility has been improved on Windows by fixing bugs where hot-tracked menu buttons and selected menu items were not firing the correct focus events for screen readers. (4440e79) (9213a81)
    AccessibilityUIWindows
  • On macOS, vector icons are being reintroduced to context menus, and web dialogs using aria-labelledby will now correctly expose their name as AXDescription to screen readers. (6e481f2) (13c61dd)
    UIAccessibilitymacOS

Build & Infrastructure

  • The //chrome/browser module continues to be modularized by splitting its main static_library into smaller, more focused source sets, improving build structure and parallelism. (6be6fe6)
    BuildBrowser
  • Unnecessary build dependencies were removed from the chrome_initial target on Windows, which can help reduce the final executable size. (6b2d03f)
    BuildWindows
  • A new UMA histogram, Antivirus.RetrievalLatency, was added on Windows to measure the time taken to retrieve antivirus product information via WMI. (86a49d1)
    SecurityWindowsInfra

Policy & Enterprise

  • A new enterprise policy, GeminiExperimentalTriggeringSettings, has been implemented to allow administrators to control experimental Gemini (AI) features. (38dcc2f)
    PolicyEnterpriseAI

2026-05-07

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-07

Overview

This digest covers 776 commits to the Chromium main branch on May 7th, 2026, with 573 commits identified as relevant to developers building on the platform. The day was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes across core APIs, extensions, networking, and the build system, requiring careful attention from embedders. The first commit of the day was 175b21588a85f119f3590b162d3f82eca74b21e6 and the last was 81c154bee3c796d05d993b89e44bf43e3ac92bd9.

Breaking API & Build Changes

  • ⚠️Multiple core browser interfaces have been modified. content::WebContents::ForSecurityDropFullscreen now returns std::optional<base::ScopedClosureRunner> to fix a UAF. TabListInterface::MoveTabGroupToWindow now returns a bool to indicate success. The bookmarks::BookmarkClient interface requires a new pure virtual method implementation: GetBookmarkFormFactor(). A recently added CreateBubble method on BubbleDialogDelegateView was reverted. (058c2d6) (daea58c) (85f5cc3) (275e4e3)
    BrowserAPIBookmarks
  • ⚠️The extensions::WebRequestInfo struct has been changed, replacing int render_process_id and int frame_routing_id with a strongly-typed content::GlobalRenderFrameHostId. Additionally, the chrome.mimeHandler.abortAndFallbackToNativeHandler() extension API was removed shortly after its introduction. (0f97ab9) (18dd6a9) (176ea56)
    ExtensionsAPI
  • ⚠️The net::URLRequest class no longer has the set_force_ignore_site_for_cookies() method; this logic is now centralized in the NetworkDelegate. In the File System API, the origin parameter has been removed from the blink::mojom::FileSystemManager::Open Mojo interface. (c0edd27) (5c6dc2f)
    NetworkStorageAPI
  • ⚠️Graphics, Media, and XR APIs saw several breaking changes. cc::LayerTreeHostSingleThreadClient was renamed to cc::LayerTreeHostSingleThreadDelegate. The tex_coord_rect field was removed from viz render pass quads. MediaRemoter::Client requires a new CreateRemotingDataStreamSender method. The content::XrInstallHelper::EnsureInstalled callback now uses an enum instead of a boolean. On macOS, the VideoCaptureProvider interface for screen capture was updated. (2b1a6ff) (871a3b5) (ef4ede0) (000d6aa) (c9e372a)
    CompositorMediaXR
  • ⚠️The accessibility event ax::mojom::Event::kFocusContext has been functionally removed, as its platform mappings have been deleted. Separately, the ReadAnythingLifecycleObserver::Activate method signature was updated with a new std::optional<base::TimeDelta> parameter. (95cdd5f) (4e9ba96)
    AccessibilityAPI
  • ⚠️A new build dependency on //third_party/llvm-libc:headers is now required by V8. Embedders of V8 or Chromium will need to add this build target to avoid compilation failures. (365c552)
    BuildV8

Security & Privacy

  • A new enterprise policy, IsolatedModeSettings, has been introduced, allowing administrators to replace the standard Incognito mode with a new "Isolated mode" profile type. (b2955dd)
    EnterprisePolicySecurity
  • A vulnerability in the PWA LaunchQueue was fixed. The system now ensures a navigation has committed before delivering File System Access handles, preventing a malicious page from intercepting them. (2375aea)
    SecurityStorage
  • A logic flaw was corrected where HTTPS-First Mode exemptions were incorrectly being applied to users with Advanced Protection enabled. (46126f9)
    SecurityNetwork
  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability when handling redirects to chrome-extension:// URLs was fixed by ensuring the Extension object is kept alive via scoped_refptr during security checks. (79f10bb)
    SecurityExtensions

API & Extensions

  • A new convenience function, SimpleMenuModel::SetIconForCommandId, has been added. This provides a more robust way to set menu item icons by command ID instead of relying on brittle index-based access. (0b02dcb)
    UIAPI
  • The SoundsManager has been moved from a ChromeOS-specific location to the more general //services/audio directory. This refactoring allows it to be reused for browser audio playback on desktop platforms like Windows and macOS. (608ac09)
    BrowserMediaAPI
  • For WebUI In-Product Help (IPH), a new containerElement option allows developers to specify a custom parent for help bubbles, which is particularly useful for displaying help within modal dialogs. (be0c292)
    UIAPI

Browser & UI

  • On macOS, a race condition was fixed where the WebUI Omnibox popup would fail to appear if invoked very quickly after opening a new window. (b6ba4ea)
    UImacOS
  • The internal implementation of the tab context menu was refactored to use tab handles instead of indices, improving robustness against race conditions. (6958f31)
    UITabs
  • The behavior of the password save dialog has been adjusted. After a user dismisses the dialog three times for a given site, the "Not now" option will no longer be shown, simplifying the UI to just "Never" and "Save". (b91fbb8)
    UIPasswordManager
  • The BrowserFocusController was refactored to use a new delegate pattern, improving its architecture and separating concerns more cleanly. (59aebd5)
    BrowserUI

Networking

  • A stability issue causing a crash in QUIC sessions with active WebSocket HTTP/3 streams was resolved. The session's stream-walking code was updated to correctly handle different stream types. (a6818ed)
    Network
  • The CacheSharingForPervasiveResources feature was enabled by default to improve resource caching performance. (4e04f86)
    NetworkPerformance

Testing & Infrastructure

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in ChromeDriver was fixed by adding proper lifetime management when handling WebAuthn automation commands. (de99d4d)
    TestingSecurity
  • A new ScopedMemoryLimitOverride utility was introduced to allow browser tests to precisely control memory limits for consumers, improving test reliability. (808dfa6)
    TestingBase
  • The feedback uploader was modified to remove a hardcoded Google URL, preventing non-Google builds from inadvertently sending feedback reports to Google's servers. (001b506)
    BuildInfra
  • On Windows, non-ASCII clipboard format names are now filtered out to prevent a CHECK failure and potential crash when interacting with the clipboard. (9cbb579)
    WindowsUI

2026-05-06

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-06

Overview

On May 6, 2026, the Chromium main branch saw 741 commits, with 558 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. This day was notable for several breaking changes impacting the Extensions API, macOS application lifecycle, and the WebUI Searchbox Mojo interface. Other key updates include a new cache-only mode for the Geolocation API, security enhancements for Web Serial and WebAuthn, and performance improvements to networking and on-device speech. The first and last commits of this period are dcf44d8d52c09b98799d69728478f7b2ce0b92ed and 056c4390d5d620fec1e33a361b631bc5b0491409.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public method extensions::MimeTypesHandler::GetHandler was renamed to Get and its signature was changed to take a const extensions::Extension& instead of a pointer. Embedder code that queries for extension MIME type handlers must be updated. (d1eb114)
    ExtensionsAPI
  • ⚠️The extensions::Extension::GetManifestData() method's return type was changed from ManifestData* to const ManifestData*. This enforces read-only access and will cause compilation errors in code that assigns the result to a non-const pointer. (9d8833a)
    ExtensionsAPI
  • ⚠️On macOS, the signatures for application termination methods in AppController have changed. The methods -tryToTerminateApplication: and -stopTryingToTerminateApplication: no longer have return types or parameters, requiring updates for embedders managing the app lifecycle. (c742828)
    macOSAPIBrowser
  • ⚠️The public searchbox.mojom interface received breaking changes. The AddDriveContext method was removed, and the OnDriveUploadClicked method signature was changed to be asynchronous. Clients of this Mojo API will need to be updated. (ea711c7) (19b3361)
    UIAPI

API & Core Interfaces

  • The Geolocation service now offers a "cache-only" mode. The new QueryCachedNextPosition method in geolocation.mojom allows callers to retrieve the last known location without triggering a new device location fetch. (2487f9f)
    APIPrivacyNetwork
  • To improve memory safety, a new std::unique_ptr-based CreateBubble method has been introduced for creating views::Widget bubbles. The older, raw-pointer-returning methods are now deprecated. (1a3139e)
    UIBase
  • The Web Serial API (navigator.serial.requestPort) now performs stricter validation on Bluetooth service class IDs, rejecting requests with invalid IDs to enhance security. (0317f43)
    APISecurityBluetooth
  • A breaking change to the AudioManager interface, which modified device enumeration methods, was introduced and then immediately reverted due to build failures. The net effect is no change to the API. (470bf4c) (8ffee52)
    MediaAPIBuild

Security

  • A use-after-free vulnerability in LegacyRenderWidgetHostHWND on Windows was fixed by adding WeakPtr guards to handle potential object destruction during synchronous Win32 calls. (73cfb55)
    WindowsSecurity
  • A use-after-free bug in the chrome.management.installReplacementWebApp API was resolved by adding a WeakPtr check to safely handle cases where a WebContents is destroyed during the installation flow. (9a50771)
    ExtensionsSecurity
  • A crash in the WebAuthn EnclaveManager was fixed to gracefully handle cases where pending keys do not match the primary signed-in user, preventing a CHECK failure. (636a107)
    SecurityWebAuthn
  • The HybridEncryptionKey component used in the sign-in flow has been migrated from direct BoringSSL calls to use the safer crypto::hpke and crypto::keypair APIs, simplifying the implementation. (3641c9e)
    CryptoSecuritySync

Browser UI & Features

  • The network service thread priority is now boosted whenever any visible page is loading, rather than only for focused pages. This change aims to improve First Contentful Paint (FCP) and Largest Contentful Paint (LCP). (809a575)
    NetworkPerformance
  • A new feature, gated by the kPreemptiveSodaDownload flag, automatically downloads the SODA (Speech on-device API) binary and language packs on startup to make features like Live Caption available immediately. (a897a50)
    MediaPerformanceAccessibility
  • A bug was fixed where command states, such as the Print menu item, would remain disabled after moving a tab with an active modal dialog (like Print Preview) to a new window. (c0f54c7)
    UITabsPrinting
  • On macOS, CoreHaptics framework interactions are now wrapped in error-handling blocks to prevent browser crashes if a game controller is disconnected while a vibration task is pending. (2b47612)
    macOSMedia
  • URLLoaderFactories are now correctly reset when the last extension requiring web request proxying is unloaded, preventing stale proxy configurations. (3bd01af)
    ExtensionsNetwork

Testing & Infrastructure

  • The WebViewFocusManager testing utility was refactored by extracting its focus tracking logic into a more reusable FocusChangeObserver class in chrome/test/base. (d68f069)
    TestingBase
  • Several circular dependencies in the build system were broken between //chrome/browser and modules like spellchecker, gpu, and segmentation_platform, improving code health. (047a726) (8b96916) (755f4c3)
    BuildInfra
  • The app_shell_unittests binary, which is no longer needed, has been removed from the build configuration. (361726b)
    BuildTesting

2026-05-05

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-05

Overview

On 2026-05-05, the Chromium main branch saw 803 commits, with 614 being relevant to developers. This digest focuses on changes impacting APIs, extensions, security, and browser UI on Windows and macOS. The first relevant commit was 3f267f3cd2d398153685b93e619d360bba561d5f and the last was 38c1ba662c38dbfde3c0fbe5a2679a751d00ebb5.

APIs & Core Interfaces

  • ⚠️Several focus management methods (e.g., FocusInactivePopupForAccessibility, RotatePaneFocus) have been removed from the public BrowserWindow interface. Embedders must now use the new BrowserFocusController class for this functionality. (16c69f3)
    BrowserUIAPI
  • ⚠️Browser shutdown and unload logic has been moved from the Browser class to a new UnloadController. This architectural change alters the public API of the Browser class, breaking embedders with custom shutdown or window closing logic. (37c7b9f)
    BrowserAPI
  • ⚠️A new virtual method, RequestImmersivePlaybackConfirmation, has been added to the content::WebContentsDelegate public interface to support a confirmation flow for immersive Picture-in-Picture. Embedders who subclass this delegate must implement the new method to avoid compilation failures. (926f5fe)
    MediaUIAPI
  • ⚠️The AutofillClient parameter in several methods has been changed from const to non-const. This is a breaking change for any embedder that implements the AutofillClient interface, as their method signatures will no longer match. (4bd9046)
    AutofillAPI
  • ⚠️The virtual method PermissionContextBase::NotifyPermissionSet has a new signature, adding an optional const content::PermissionResult* parameter. Subclasses that override this method must be updated to match. (5f2014a)
    PrivacyAPI
  • ⚠️On macOS, several synchronous public methods on TextInputClientMac have been renamed with a Sync prefix (e.g., characterIndexForPoint is now SyncCharacterIndexForPoint). This is a breaking API change for macOS embedders directly using this class. (60aa06c)
    macOSUI
  • To prevent database corruption, the SQLite VFS layer now includes CHECKs that disallow opening multiple unsynchronized connections to the same database file from the same process. This will become a fatal crash in a future milestone (M151), so developers should audit their database connection patterns. (87e5fd4) (92e25b0)
    StorageBase

GPU & Compositor

  • ⚠️The core rendering method viz::DirectRenderer::DrawFrame has a new required parameter, const TrackedElementRects&. Embedders who call this low-level rendering function directly must update their code to provide this argument. (e14bcb1)
    CompositorGPU
  • ⚠️The public API SharedImageBackingFactory::CreateSharedImage now accepts a gpu::SharedImageInfo struct instead of a long list of individual parameters. This breaks any custom implementations or direct calls to this factory method. (c06e765)
    GPUCompositor
  • ⚠️The core compositor interface LayerTreeHostSchedulingClient has been renamed to LayerTreeHostSchedulingDelegate. Embedders providing a custom implementation for LayerTreeHost initialization must update their code to the new class name. (53e1246)
    CompositorAPI
  • ⚠️The behavior of GpuPersistentCache::LoadData and MemoryCache::ReadData has changed. They now return 0 if the provided buffer is smaller than the cached data, whereas they previously may have performed a partial copy. Code expecting partial reads will be broken. (9a61b9b)
    GPUStorage

Extensions

  • ⚠️A major behavioral change now treats MIME handler extension frames (e.g., for PDFs) as the effective top-level context for network and storage partitioning. This alters how StorageKey and SiteForCookies are calculated and can break extensions that rely on the previous partitioning behavior. (45b03bb)
    ExtensionsNetworkStorage
  • ⚠️The extensions::mojom::EventRouter interface has a breaking signature change for service worker event listener registration. The service_worker_context is now passed as a separate parameter, requiring updates to existing mojom clients. (907c3bf)
    ExtensionsServiceWorker
  • ⚠️The public extension APIs extensions::CreateExtensionURLLoaderFactory and extensions::AllowCrossRendererResourceLoad have been updated for type safety. The child_id parameter was changed from a raw int to the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId, requiring code updates. (d348d44)
    ExtensionsNetwork
  • ⚠️The extensions::MimeTypesHandler::GetHandler() method now returns a const pointer. This will cause compilation failures for any code that was previously modifying the returned Handler object. (a086295)
    Extensions

Browser & UI

  • ⚠️In the PWA installation flow, the protected virtual method OnOkButtonClicked in WebAppInstallFlowDialogDelegate has been renamed to AdvanceToNextStepOrClose. This will break any custom classes that inherit from this delegate and override the original method. (50801f9)
    UIBrowser
  • The modern, FlexLayout-based infobar UI is now permanently enabled. The kInfobarRefresh feature flag and all related legacy code have been removed, making this a non-optional change to the visual appearance of all infobars. (69e4d33) (7e49209) (92a80ce) (29c1235)
    UIBrowser

Security & Network

  • ⚠️The Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) API has been updated with a callback-based approach and more granular error types. The RegistrationFetcher::Start method signature has changed, breaking existing integrations. (ab51910)
    SecurityNetworkAPI
  • The Web Share API has been enhanced to perform URL validation in the browser process. This prevents a compromised renderer from bypassing renderer-side checks to share local file:// URLs, mitigating a potential information disclosure vector. (3f267f3)
    SecurityPrivacy
  • To mitigate Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) risks, the allowed URL schemes for PWA manifest icons have been restricted. PWAs using schemes other than http, https, or data for their icons may no longer install or update correctly. (7cb6a9b)
    SecurityUIBrowser

Build & Infrastructure

  • The app_shell_unittests build target has been removed from public build configurations as a step toward the complete removal of the deprecated app_shell binary. Embedders who used this for testing may need to find alternative solutions. (28e3d31)
    BuildTestingExtensions

2026-05-04

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-04

Overview

This summary covers 427 relevant commits out of 567 total commits on the main branch for May 4th, 2026. This was a significant day for API maintenance, with numerous breaking changes across the base, ui, compositor, and password_manager components. The first relevant commit was 162a1baff86124785cc799c259c045bc314bd6e0 and the last was 4802e283b7554878f55669bc90ec0e5793a67ec5.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️Public API Removal: The PasswordStoreConsumer interface now uses StoredCredential instead of PasswordForm. Methods like OnGetPasswordStoreResultsOrErrorFrom now provide a LoginsResult (a vector of StoredCredential), breaking consumers that expect the legacy PasswordForm struct. (2b93d3b)
    PasswordManagerAPI
  • ⚠️Public API Removal: The function base::subtle::Atomic64::Acquire_Load has been removed from base/atomicops.h. Downstream projects using this specific atomic operation will no longer compile. (ef2ad96)
    Base
  • ⚠️Public API Removal: The public interface DesktopBrowserWindowCapabilitiesDelegate has been removed from chrome/browser/ui/browser_window/public/. Any embedder directly using or implementing this delegate will be broken. (858b736)
    BrowserUI
  • ⚠️Public API Rename: The core compositor interface cc::LayerTreeHostImplClient has been renamed to cc::LayerTreeHostImplDelegate. Embedders interacting with the compositor thread must update to the new name. (67cf5cf)
    CompositorGPU
  • ⚠️Public API Signature Change: The BrowserAutofillManager::DidShowSuggestions() method signature has changed. It now accepts a FormGlobalId instead of a FormData object, requiring updates for subclasses or direct callers. (50dc42f)
    AutofillAPI
  • ⚠️Public API Signature Change: The method SetVSyncDisplayID in gl::Presenter, viz::OutputSurface, and viz::SkiaOutputDevice now requires a force_update boolean parameter. Embedders subclassing these interfaces must update their implementations. (a5ab2ae)
    GPUCompositormacOS
  • ⚠️Public API Signature Change: The API for showing the Cast dialog is now asynchronous. Methods in CastDialogCoordinator and MediaRouterDialogControllerViews now require an AfterShownCallback, breaking programmatic invocations of the dialog. (982b0cc)
    MediaUI
  • ⚠️Public API Removal: The views::BubbleDialogDelegate::CreateBubble static method has been renamed to CreateBubbleDeprecated. This is a hard rename that will break callsites, not a soft deprecation. (5ee0e6a)
    UI
  • ⚠️Public API Removal: The methods SetShouldShowLeadingSeparator() and SetShouldShowTrailingSeparator() have been removed from the MultiContentsView class, as they are no longer used by the side panel. (04ff39d)
    UIBrowser
  • ⚠️Public API Removal: The class template base::trace_event::TraceScopedTrackableObject has been removed from base/trace_event/trace_event.h as part of a migration away from legacy tracing macros. (00b4862)
    BaseInfra

Browser UI & Features

  • The PWA installation dialog for macOS has been implemented, and its progress bar logic was updated to reflect actual installation progress rather than being time-based. (c9dcfdf) (e3a6456)
    BrowsermacOSUI
  • The views::ScopedPaintLock utility was improved to more reliably defer all paint scheduling within a locked view's subtree, preventing unnecessary invalidations from propagating up the view hierarchy and improving performance. (8ab1310)
    UIPerformance
  • For WebAuthn, the interstitial shown in incognito mode when creating a Google Password Manager passkey has been removed to streamline the user experience. (a63811e)
    SecurityPasswordManagerUI
  • Support for autorevoking approximate and precise geolocation permissions was added to Safety Hub. This involved refactoring to store and manage the granularity of the revoked permission. (2b90bb7)
    SecurityPrivacyUI

Security & Privacy

  • A Use-After-Free in FileSelectHelper on macOS was fixed by improving its memory management and lifetime handling. (2e5d150)
    SecuritymacOS
  • A Use-After-Free in the Windows HID implementation (HidConnectionWin) was addressed by ensuring asynchronous transfer objects remain valid until their completion callbacks are invoked. (907bec0)
    SecurityWindows
  • A logic error in the Safe Browsing DMGAnalyzer was fixed. Nested archives within DMG files are now correctly submitted for analysis instead of being skipped. (d942e1c)
    SecuritymacOS
  • The ContentAnnotatorService now observes history deletions and cancels in-progress annotations for URLs that have been removed, preventing annotation data from being cached for deleted pages. (aba42a3)
    SecurityHistory
  • A Use-After-Free in DeviceAuthenticatorMac was fixed by using weak pointers to handle nested run loops that can occur during authentication dialogs. (7a43db7)
    SecuritymacOS

Media & GPU

  • The CastStreamingMediaVideoEncoder feature has been enabled by default. A related bug causing silent encoder failures on macOS was also fixed. (ee0ca84) (661946c)
    MediamacOS
  • The reliability of CADisplayLink on macOS was improved by implementing a fallback that switches its operation from the GPU process to the browser process after power or refresh rate changes. (739308a)
    GPUmacOS

Testing & Infrastructure

  • Build Target Removal: The extensions_browsertests build target has been removed. All relevant integration tests have been migrated into the main browser_tests binary as part of a major cleanup of the extensions testing infrastructure. (3df42d8)
    ExtensionsTestingBuild
  • Helper functions for managing SQL tables were promoted from the Autofill component into the general sql/ directory for broader use across Chromium. (a6cbd90) (548b964)
    BaseStorage
  • A bug in the bundled minizip library was fixed. The handling of the "Unicode Path Extra Field" was corrected to prevent it from overriding filename sizes, which could cause the library to skip files when parsing a zip archive's central directory. (21217a0)
    Infra

2026-05-03

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-03

Overview

This digest covers 22 commits, with a focus on changes relevant to Windows and macOS embedders. After filtering out platform-specific (iOS, Android, Ash) and internal rendering engine changes, several key updates were identified in Autofill, browser internals, and networking. The most notable change is a breaking API modification in the Autofill component, which removes a virtual method from a public class.

Autofill

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The BrowserAutofillManager::DidShowSuggestions() method was changed from virtual to non-virtual. This is a breaking API change for any downstream code that inherits from BrowserAutofillManager and overrides this method, as it will now cause a compilation failure. The intended pattern is now to use the AutofillManager::Observer::OnSuggestionsShown() callback. (bc91d7f)

Browser Internals

  • The UnloadController, which manages the browser closing process (e.g., beforeunload events), was refactored. It is no longer a direct member of the Browser class but is now owned by BrowserWindowFeatures. Access patterns have been updated to use UnloadController::From(browser). While a significant internal change, the public API of the Browser class for closing remains functionally unchanged. (beaae0f)
  • A bug fix in the Windows touch keyboard implementation now correctly respects the autocorrect="off" HTML attribute, preventing unwanted text replacements. This aligns behavior with web standards. (02d88f7)

Networking & Storage

  • Internal optimizations were made to the Off-Main-Thread Prefetch (OMTPrefetch) mechanism to deduplicate in-flight tasks and improve the header cache refresh logic, enhancing performance and reliability of preloading. (13008e3) (c83f29a)
  • A bug in the sandboxed SQLite Virtual File System (VFS) was fixed. The VFS now correctly treats zero-length files as non-existent, aligning with the behavior of built-in VFSes and preventing issues with journal and write-ahead log file handling. (c14174a)

Infrastructure

  • Minor updates include a comment clarification in ui/views/scoped_paint_lock.h and a routine update to internationalization string resources. (5e2ce33) (8f666cc)

2026-05-02

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-02

Overview

This digest covers 47 commits from (c45c88b) to (94371de). After filtering for changes relevant to Windows and macOS embedders and excluding areas like Blink, V8, and platform-specific mobile code, 20 commits were analyzed. Key themes include security hardening for extensions and navigation, UI refinements for the new tab page and vertical tabs, and fixes for platform integration on macOS and Windows.

Security

  • Security for service worker event listeners in extensions has been enhanced. New validation checks in the EventRouter ensure that IPC messages to add or remove listeners originate from an authorized process, preventing a compromised renderer from spoofing these requests. (31dce68)
  • A potential vulnerability in subframe error handling has been addressed. Previously, an error page for a subframe could be created with an origin derived from the destination URL. Now, error pages that commit in the current process are forced to use a new, opaque unique origin, mitigating risks associated with a compromised renderer forcing a commit with a victim's precursor. (470a561)
  • The on-device model service now includes additional safety flags for model assets being passed to the sandbox, improving the security posture of this feature. (ebf0ace)

Extensions

  • A crash in the EventRouter has been fixed. The crash occurred when an extension tried to remove a stale, in-memory event listener whose filter was no longer present in persisted preferences. The router now gracefully handles this case by checking for the filter's existence before attempting removal. (ce6c6b2)

Browser UI & Features

  • As part of the ongoing UI refresh, the feature flag for the WebUI toolbar has been renamed from kWebUIRefresh2026 to kWebUIFlexibleToolbar. (c45c88b)
  • The New Tab Page has been updated to remove shortcuts by default, aiming for a simpler default experience. (3a36117)
  • A visual enhancement for vertical tabs with a transparent 'glass' frame has been implemented. The tab strip will now correctly fade to the glass background, improving animations during collapse and expansion. (89fdd0f)
  • A bug causing full-page blanking during screenshot redaction has been fixed. The logic now correctly copies the original bitmap before drawing redaction masks, ensuring the rest of the page remains visible. (c1c7fa4)

Platform Integration & Devices

  • On macOS, a bug that could lead to uninitialized memory access when setting gamepad vibration has been fixed. The code now correctly checks if a force feedback effect was successfully created before attempting to use it, preventing potential crashes. (b90a779)
  • The Chromium updater UI on Windows now supports high contrast modes, improving accessibility by dynamically adopting system colors for its progress window. (3517cbe)
  • An internal refactoring on macOS has removed a manual visibility tracking field in NativeWidgetNSWindowBridge, replacing it with a direct call to the system's -[NSWindow isVisible] method. (91a1300)

Internals & Infrastructure

  • The Widevine test license server infrastructure, which was Linux-only and difficult to maintain, has been removed from the codebase and DEPS. Encrypted media tests that relied on it are now updated to expect failure or be skipped, aligning behavior with other platforms. (1262aed)
  • PWA browser tests have been made more robust by replacing flaky heuristics with a new WebAppPageWaiter utility. This utility uses a new event on WebAppTabHelper to reliably determine when all web app system activity is scheduled for a given page. (4b3f90b)
  • A bug in the GPU command buffer decoder has been fixed. The decoder will no longer prematurely set a texture's 'cleared' flag if the underlying texture allocation fails due to a GL error. (f786ca0)
  • The build system has been refactored to move preference-related files for the accessibility annotator component into their own dedicated prefs build target. (9ca9e21)

2026-05-01

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-01

Overview

This daily summary covers 603 commits on May 1st, 2026, with 431 being relevant to developers building on Chromium. The day saw numerous significant breaking changes to core APIs, security policies, and UI components, requiring attention from embedders. The first commit of the day was 2b2726ca9d5289a827b6802d96192467d2aa6caa and the last was 22921c2492c410545d874d82e16946f48c678ad5.

APIs and Core Internals

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core compositor class cc::LayerTreeHostClient has been renamed to cc::LayerTreeHostDelegate. Embedders interacting directly with the compositor layer must update class names and includes to fix compile-time errors. (afd22cd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public C++ API for speech recognition has been refactored. Delegate methods and SpeechRecognitionSessionContext now use content::GlobalRenderFrameHostId instead of separate render_process_id and render_frame_id integers, breaking existing implementations. (c15d856) (5ee42da)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core browser navigation headers, including browser_navigator.h and browser_navigator_params.h, have been moved into a new chrome/browser/ui/navigator/ subdirectory. This is a build-breaking change requiring include paths to be updated. (815c5cf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The default Google crash report server URL has been removed for non-branded builds. Embedders must now explicitly configure their own crash reporting endpoint to continue receiving crash reports. (e7f4186)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The is_ok() method has been removed from media::TypedStatus templates where a status type has no defined "OK" state. This is a compile-time breaking change for code that was incorrectly calling this method. (5d969f6)
  • The BrowserManagerService is now instantiated for all Profile types, including those that do not support Browser objects. This change simplifies consumer code by removing the need for null-checks when querying browser state. (9fadf12)
  • The chrome.histograms WebUI API has been improved to accept floating-point numbers, which are now automatically rounded to the nearest integer. This prevents runtime exceptions when using high-precision timers like performance.now(). (1622742)

Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The browser will now terminate a renderer process if it sends invalid navigation headers. This escalates the response from a non-fatal report to a CHECK, representing a major behavioral change in security enforcement that may affect renderer stability. (e0a0f3e)
  • A potential Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability on Windows has been fixed in the Chrome Remote Desktop Host. The service now prevents following directory junctions and applies stricter ACLs to its configuration directory. (f67fb61)
  • A security flaw in the MHTML FrameSerializer has been patched. The serializer now correctly handles mixed-case scripting attributes (e.g., ONLOAD), preventing a potential bypass. (19cb047)

UI and UX

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public C++ API for views::BubbleDialogDelegate::CreateBubble has been changed by removing the default ownership parameter. All callers must now explicitly specify the widget ownership (e.g., views::Widget::InitParams::CLIENT_OWNS_WIDGET), causing a compile-time failure for code relying on the old implicit default. (3301350)
  • A bug causing hangs when detaching tabs has been fixed by making the tab drag controller use tab drag data as the source of truth. A separate fix addresses a use-after-free crash when dragging vertical tabs. (9000f56) (5780c24)
  • On macOS, a bug that caused Cmd+KeyUp events to be swallowed by the application has been fixed. These events are now correctly forwarded to the key window, improving keyboard shortcut reliability. (7086937)

Enterprise and Policy

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The enterprise policy ForceForegroundPriorityForOrigins has been renamed to ForceForegroundPriorityForUrls. The policy's behavior is also changed from matching origins to matching full URLs, requiring administrators to update their configurations. (605fbfc)
  • A new enterprise policy, SecuritySignalsClientCertificatesSelectors, has been added. This allows administrators to configure the collection of client certificate details for security reporting purposes. (10dd81c)

Networking

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The deprecated HashValue(base::span<const uint8_t>) constructor has been removed from net/base/hash_value.h. Code must be updated to use the HashValue(HashValueTag, base::span) constructor instead. (a91b8f4)
  • A base::Feature flag has been added to enable experimental support for ML-DSA signatures in TLS. This is part of the ongoing effort to integrate post-quantum cryptographic algorithms. (a2638b6)
  • Progress continues on the new SQL-based HTTP cache. The SqlSharedCacheIndexDatabase component was implemented to map a NetworkIsolationKey to a unique ID for isolating cache data. (7a02630)

Extensions

  • The extension EventRouter now performs stricter validation on IPC messages for adding or removing event listeners. This hardening prevents a compromised renderer from spoofing messages to modify listeners for an extension it shouldn't control. (6271562)
  • The runtime.getPlatformInfo() API has been updated to formally deprecate the nacl_arch field, reflecting the removal of Native Client (NaCl) from Chromium. (6f14e2a)

Performance

  • The history loading path for omnibox suggestions has been significantly optimized. The code now batch-fetches recent visits in a single SQL query instead of potentially thousands of separate queries, improving startup and omnibox performance. (3ed049f)

2026-04-30

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-30

Overview

This digest covers 745 commits on the main branch from commit (084453c) to (5186844), with 556 identified as relevant to developers. This cycle introduces numerous significant breaking changes to the Remoting component, network observers, PWA manifest handling, and several core component APIs. Other notable updates include enabling Vertical Tabs by default, modernizing the extension MIME handler architecture, and patching several security vulnerabilities.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Remoting: The SessionConfig and CandidateSessionConfig classes have been removed, enforcing WebRTC as the sole transport protocol. This removes public APIs like SessionManager::set_protocol_config() and ConnectionToHost::config(), requiring embedders to update their remoting implementations. (32d22ac)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Networking: A new method, OnPlatformLocalNetworkPermissionRequired, has been added to the URLLoaderNetworkServiceObserver mojom interface. Embedders must implement this method to handle new OS-level permission prompts for the Local Network Protection (LNP) feature, otherwise local network requests may fail. (3218389)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Web Applications (PWAs): The webapps::ManifestId type has been changed from a GURL alias to webapps::ValidManifestId, which wraps an std::optional<GURL>. This enforces validation at the type level, requiring code that uses ManifestId to handle potential invalid IDs and access the GURL via .value(). (73319ea)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Enterprise Policy: The tie-breaking logic for the PrefURLListMatcher component has been altered. It now prioritizes the longest host *before* the longest path when evaluating multiple matching URL patterns. This can change how existing enterprise policies are applied. (806b0e9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Compositor: Several public methods, including CreateUIResourceFromImportedResource, have been moved from the base class cc::LayerTreeHostImpl to the derived class viz::VizLayerTreeHostImpl. Embedders calling these methods on the base class will need to update their code. (a0e7321)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Components: The PageContentExtractionService::Observer::OnPageContentExtracted method signature has changed. It now passes a page_content_annotations::PageContent variant instead of a scoped_refptr. Existing observers must be updated to handle the new type. (2f7a652)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Extensions: The webstorePrivate API permission and its associated feature definitions have been completely removed. This API was used by the legacy Chrome Web Store hosted app and is no longer accessible. (de56b30)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Base Library: The public function base::DateIntervalFormat and the associated base::DateFormat enum have been removed from base/i18n/time_formatting.h. Code relying on this for formatting date intervals must be updated. (1c49b4d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Components: The public method SendTabToSelfModel::AddEntry has been renamed to SendEntry. Embedders using the components/send_tab_to_self API must update their method calls. (2576012)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Sync: The device_info_specifics.proto protocol buffer has been modified, replacing the glic_experimental_triggering_opted_in boolean field with an enum. This is a breaking change for any client that directly parses this sync proto. (2beaaac)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Mojom: The signature of the public mojom interface FrameWidgetInputHandler::PasteFromImageBytes has been changed, removing its bool success callback. Clients calling this method will need to be updated as it no longer provides a result. (6f76ae0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Components: The public methods Normalize() and Magnitude() have been removed from the passage_embeddings::Embedding class. Consumers of this components/passage_embeddings API must now ensure embeddings are normalized before use. (9d9fbd3)

Browser & Core Components

  • A new dictation component has been added to the browser, introducing a SessionController for managing dictation sessions. (3d5eec5)
  • The migration from the deprecated base::MemoryPressureListener to the new base::MemoryConsumer API continues, with ProgramCache and OnDeviceTailModelService being updated. This gives memory management policies more flexibility. (1c39e4f) (6c95781)
  • The logic for Page Actions has been refactored out of Browser and into a new, more modular chrome/browser/ui/page_actions component. (4f093f4)
  • The 'Bedrock' initiative continues with large-scale refactorings to migrate code from Browser* to the new BrowserWindowInterface, improving modularity. (dc981f9) (5ed392f) (b5fd10b)

UI & UX

  • The Vertical Tabs feature is now enabled by default on Windows and macOS. Follow-up changes improve drag-and-drop visuals and behavior. (20e810a) (bee36ee) (35a0a9a)
  • A new utility, views::ScopedPaintLock, has been introduced. It allows developers to temporarily block and defer paint updates within a View and its children, which is useful for preventing unnecessary paints during complex UI updates. (9e609b6)
  • On macOS, the compositor is now 'warmed up' when a window is about to become visible, reducing latency and the potential for hangs during window activation. (ae5a92b)
  • On Windows 11, PWA window caption buttons now support Snap Layouts on hover by returning the correct HTMAXBUTTON hit-test value. (9ceea0d)
  • For accessibility, text selection events for native views are now fired on the node itself rather than as a document-level event, improving the experience for assistive technologies. (4b19c4f) (0525c9b)

Extensions

  • The architecture for how extensions handle MIME types is being modernized. A new chrome.mimeHandler.getStreamInfo() API has been defined and internal delegates were updated to route generic MIME handlers through the OOPIF path, improving security and performance. (d680baa) (e8aa93f) (db42fd6)
  • The stable Tab Strip API has been enhanced with new moveTab and moveCollection methods. Concurrently, the experimental WebUI-based Tab Strip feature and its associated context menu API were removed. (f7ccd6c) (17de746)
  • A Content Security Policy (CSP) bypass in the Manifest V2 enforcer was fixed. The sanitizer now correctly processes CSP Level 3 directives like script-src-elem, preventing insecure inline script execution. (99e2591)
  • To prevent cross-renderer interference, webRequest listener removals are now scoped to the originating renderer process. (be668fb)
  • The metricsPrivate.getHistogram API has been restricted via an allowlist, effectively making it a private API not intended for general use. (7446ed8)

Networking & Security

  • A security flaw in the Web Share API was patched. An HTTPS page can no longer use a <base href="file:///"> tag to bypass scheme restrictions and share file: URLs. (65fe959)
  • A bug was fixed on macOS where DRM-protected video could be screen-recorded. The video layer's preventsCapture property is now correctly updated when it is recycled to display protected content. (51a8376)
  • A logic error in insecure download blocking was fixed. The code was incorrectly using bitwise operations on PageTransition core types, which could allow some malicious downloads to bypass mixed-content blocking. (0b7e6fe)
  • The Out-Of-Renderer CORS implementation was updated to allow the Sec-GPC (Global Privacy Control) request header with a value of "1", aligning with privacy standards. (95e3466)
  • A crash in the WebAuthn Google Password Manager (GPM) flow was fixed, improving the stability of passkey recovery on desktop platforms. (cddebef)

Testing & Build System

  • A new test helper, ScopedGlobalFeaturesOverride, was added to simplify the management of global features within tests. (f6607e7)
  • The GlobalBrowserCollection is now mockable, simplifying tests that have a dependency on it. (5507db0) (fa31f88)
  • The Inspector Protocol, which powers DevTools, was rolled to a new version. This can potentially impact third-party tools and automation scripts that rely on it. (ca1f40f)

2026-04-29

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-29

Overview

This summary covers 518 relevant commits out of 699 total commits on the main branch for April 29, 2026. The day saw a significant number of breaking API changes affecting fullscreen management, the base library, password management, and session restore. Notable updates include the default use of BoringSSL for random number generation, a new sandbox for WebNN on Windows, and API improvements for extensions and core browser features. The first relevant commit was 9bc9e6ba1d... and the last was 7eab0f42e....

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Fullscreen management has been refactored out of the core BrowserWindow public interface. Methods like IsFullscreen(), EnterFullscreen(), and ExitFullscreen() are now part of the BrowserWindowFullscreenController class. Embedders must update their code to use the new controller, for example: BrowserWindowFullscreenController::From(browser)->IsFullscreen(). (d1db08e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Two public APIs have been removed from the base library. base::RandInt is removed; developers must migrate to base::RandIntInclusive. Additionally, the static method base::SequencedTaskRunner::GetCurrentBestEffort() has been removed as part of a task scheduling refactor. (5f591f6) (440e033)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public password_manager::PasswordStoreConsumer interface has been simplified by removing the obsolete OnGetPasswordStoreResultsFrom and OnGetPasswordFormList virtual methods. Separately, the SessionCommand class now requires an os_crypt_async::Encryptor when calling Serialize() and Deserialize(), breaking direct usage of these methods. (18f3b6c) (009a939)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public function net::x509_util::CalculateSha256SpkiHash no longer returns a bool and now returns the hash value directly, crashing on failure. The blink::mojom::RTCLoggingDispatcher::FinishDiagnosticLogging mojo method now requires a new metadata parameter. (7755f5d) (8e0ea31)

Browser & Core APIs

  • The TabInterface now includes a GetProfile() method for direct profile access from a tab object. A const version of MaybeGetFromContents() was also added to improve const-correctness. (8664b45) (fca1cea)
  • The CookieControlsController has been refactored from being tab-scoped to browser-scoped. This change reduces memory usage by avoiding redundant observers and caches for background tabs. (1b18a0a)
  • The SharedImageFactory::CreateSharedImage method signature was simplified to accept a SharedImageInfo struct instead of a long list of parameters. In the base library, the unmaintained MADV_FREE-based discardable memory implementation for POSIX has been completely removed. (08ee0a7) (73c9221)

Security

  • The kUseBoringSSLForRandBytes feature is now enabled by default on Windows and POSIX systems. This makes BoringSSL the primary source for random numbers generated via base::RandBytes, centralizing the implementation. (be05a9a)
  • A new sandbox type, kWebNNModelCompilation, has been introduced on Windows. This isolates the WebNN model compilation utility process from the GPU process, reducing its privileges and enhancing security. (1adb86c)
  • The Elevation Service on Windows now uses the securely installed application GUID from HKLM to verify the browser version, preventing potential spoofing via RPC. In DevTools, a logic flaw that could leak raw header access was fixed, and navigation requests are now sanitized to block access to internal URLs. (a189150) (b1cb06e) (3ea2ffb)

Networking

  • The Connection-Allowlist HTTP header now supports the webrtc=block parameter. When present, the browser will prevent the creation of RTCPeerConnection objects for the document, effectively disabling WebRTC. (ec55185)
  • URL-Keyed Metrics (UKM) will now record the full redirect chain for navigations (up to 20), providing more detailed data for analysis. To improve scrolling smoothness, the network service thread priority boost will no longer be applied during scroll events. (0f5e8b9) (c8bf2ff)

Extensions

  • The Omnibox search provider now respects the AllowDeletingBrowserHistory enterprise policy. If the policy is active, users will be prevented from deleting history suggestions directly from the Omnibox. (99f1108)
  • A use-after-free vulnerability in UserScriptSet::UpdateUserScripts has been fixed. Additionally, the extensions ProcessMap has been migrated to use the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId instead of raw integers, improving type safety. (fe455e2) (2686e85)
  • A bug has been fixed that caused the media picker dialog (e.g., for screen sharing) to be positioned incorrectly when launched from an extension's popup menu, which could lead to rendering artifacts. (2e0a6db)

2026-04-28

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-28

Overview

This summary covers 553 relevant commits out of 697 total on the main branch for April 28, 2026. The day's changes include a large number of breaking API changes affecting core utilities, networking, UI, and permissions. Notable new features include a "Smart Restart" mechanism on macOS and UI improvements for PWAs and tab management. The first relevant commit was a1d0d57548d97d843a07e906f5a7d933ee29ed0a and the last was 41d95dc2830218c18337e0c4c87f5bc700d38ba3.

API & Core Libraries

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The browser shutdown function browser_shutdown::ShutdownPreThreadsStop() now returns a browser_shutdown::RestartMode enum instead of a bool to support new restart behaviors like the "Smart Restart" feature. (7002096)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The base::LaunchProcess utility on Windows now opens the console handle with read-write access ("w+"). This alters how child processes detect a TTY and may break applications that relied on the previous write-only behavior. (6aaefd2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The ContentBrowserClient::FinishRtcDiagnosticLogging virtual method signature has changed, requiring embedders to add a new metadata parameter to their implementations. (05bd958)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A new pure virtual method, OnPasswordFormsParsed, has been added to the password_manager::PasswordManagerInterface::Observer interface. All implementations of this observer must be updated. (c20c983)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The media::VideoColorSpace class is now immutable. Direct member access (e.g., color_space.primaries) has been replaced with accessor methods (e.g., color_space.primaries()), breaking code that directly read or modified its state. (565307b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public PushMessagingService::SubscriptionInfoCallback signature has been changed to include a new user_visible_only boolean parameter. Callback implementations must be updated. (23f0c1d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Core graphics interfaces now pass gfx::CALayerParams and gpu::SwapBuffersCompleteParams by move instead of by const reference. Custom implementations of viz::OutputSurfaceClient and ui::AcceleratedWidgetMacNSView will need to be updated. (750af5d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The media::MojoRendererService::Initialize method has been hardened to prevent re-initialization. A second call will now be treated as a bad message and terminate the renderer process. (2dfbd25)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature for the public virtual method PdfInkModuleClient::DrawText() has changed to use an InkTextBoxAttributes struct, breaking existing implementations. (ee5efb8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Supervised user source files have been moved from chrome/browser/ to a new chrome/browser/supervised_user/ component. Build systems with hardcoded include paths will break. (f5f345d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The constructor for enterprise::connectors::FilesRequestHandler has changed due to a refactor. Code that directly instantiates this class must be updated. (18e969b)

Security & Permissions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The core permissions model has been updated to allow a one-time (ephemeral) and a persistent permission grant to exist simultaneously for the same origin. Custom UI or logic assuming only one grant type can be active at a time will need to be updated. (3d9fd6e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The core utility base::DeletePathRecursively on Windows will no longer traverse reparse points (e.g., directory junctions). This is a security fix that prevents accidental deletion of content outside the intended directory, but it breaks any tools that relied on the previous, unsafe behavior. (aed056d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: On Windows, ui::OpenFileViaShell now launches files using the explorer's token instead of inheriting the browser's. This is a significant behavioral change to prevent privilege escalation and will impact embedders relying on the previous token inheritance. (a68adec)
  • A vulnerability where a specially named file ('*') in a RAR archive could bypass Safe Browsing analysis has been fixed. (ac02d64)
  • Clipboard handling code on Windows was made more robust to prevent issues when processing malformed FILEGROUPDESCRIPTOR data. (6ad66e6)
  • A Use-After-Free (UAF) condition in NativeWidgetNSWindowBridge on macOS that could occur during fullscreen transitions has been fixed. (bc8da34)

Networking

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The net::URLRequest::Delegate interface has a new required pure virtual method, OnPlatformLocalNetworkAccessPermissionRequired. All embedders implementing this core networking delegate must add the new method. (0abb379)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The core networking class net::HashValue is now immutable. A tag-only constructor has been removed, and the non-const span() method was also removed, breaking any code that constructed or modified a HashValue in place. (335a74c) (8823457)
  • The TCP socket pool limit for proxies, controlled by the kTcpSocketPoolProxyLimit feature, is now enabled by default, and the default limit has been increased from 32 to 128. (3564124)
  • A new navigation throttle has been added to attach the X-Geo HTTP header to Google-bound navigation requests on all platforms. (c8b8e95)

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The autofillPrivate extensions API will now return an empty list instead of an error when its data managers are not yet loaded. This changes an error condition to a success condition, which can break logic in extensions that relied on the previous error-handling behavior. (b1e55a8)

Browser UI & Features

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The OmniboxAction struct within the public toolbar_ui_api_data_model.mojom interface has been refactored into a union. This is a breaking change for any client using this mojom API. (92e6fc8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The "card-linked offers" feature has been removed from Autofill. This includes changing the signature of the public function CreateCreditCardSuggestion, which will break any code calling it directly. (10d5999)
  • A new "Smart Restart" feature has been introduced on macOS to apply pending updates silently when no browser windows are open. A new public function chrome::AttemptRestartWithMode is available to trigger different restart types. (7002096)
  • The "Tabs From Other Devices" side panel now includes a search box to allow users to filter tabs by title or URL. (3186cd6)
  • The PWA installation dialog on Windows has been updated to include checkboxes for creating a desktop shortcut and pinning the app to the taskbar. (6f7528b)
  • When a video screen capture is aborted on macOS (e.g., via the native "Stop Sharing" button), any associated audio capture will now also be terminated. (fc933fa)

2026-04-27

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-27

Overview

This summary covers 571 commits to the Chromium main branch on April 27, 2026, with 427 identified as relevant to developers. The day saw a significant number of breaking changes, particularly in security-related APIs like unexportable keys, extension behaviors, and core graphics and capture interfaces. Key commits range from 398bf4389292a344f5bdbfcc196ee3f723af36bc to 34d0ce4055b12f4b3049705d22eb7cfe5285250f.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Unexportable Keys API was significantly refactored, changing public method signatures and class hierarchies in the crypto library. This includes renaming DeleteSigningKeysSlowly to DeleteKeysSlowly, introducing UnexportableKey as a base class, and replacing AsStatefulUnexportableSigningKey() with AsStatefulKey(). Consumers of the Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) feature must update their code. (e94b66b) (2763d6c) (65a9d85)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A strict same-origin policy is now enforced for SharedWorkers created by extensions and Isolated Web Apps. This security fix will terminate the renderer if violated and may break extensions that were incorrectly using cross-origin scripts. (0e9234d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The terminalPrivate.onProcessOutput() extension API event is now dispatched only to the sender renderer that opened the process, instead of being broadcast to all renderers. Extensions relying on the previous broadcast behavior will break. (4d9c2be)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The chrome.virtualKeyboard and chrome.virtualKeyboardPrivate extension APIs have been removed from non-ChromeOS platforms (Windows, Mac, Linux). (ac2eccb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The viz::CopyOutputBitmapWithMetadata struct, returned by the widely used RenderWidgetHostView::CopyFromSurface method, has a new TrackedElementRects member and a modified constructor. Embedders using this API for surface capture must update their code. (3bf7300)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Web Feed (Following) feature has been completely removed from the browser, including the web_feed_subscriptions C++ module and the WEB_FEED_SORT feature flag. (3c759c0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several DNS-over-HTTPS preference keys (e.g., kDnsOverHttpsMode, kDnsOverHttpsTemplates) have been removed from the common chrome/common/pref_names.h file and are no longer available on non-ChromeOS platforms. (be15c40)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public function CreateDetailedInstallDialogView has been removed from chrome/browser/ui/web_applications/web_app_dialogs.h. Code calling this function to create PWA installation dialogs will no longer compile. (b2ccbf3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for PasswordStoreBackend::RemoveLoginsCreatedBetweenAsync has been changed, removing the sync_completion callback parameter. Embedders with custom PasswordStoreBackend implementations must update their method signature. (a0f57d1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The glRequestExtensionCHROMIUM command buffer API has been hardened and now validates requests against a strict allowlist. This may break clients that were requesting extensions not on the new pre-validated list. (7bdc81e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Windows, the remoting::mojom::ChromotingHostServices interface implementation was moved to the daemon process and its BindChromotingHostServices method signature was changed, breaking direct integrations. (e882156)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for cc::ScrollUpdateEventMetrics::Create and cc::ScrollEventMetrics::Create in the compositor (cc) library has been changed to require a new scroll_begin_arrival_timestamp argument. (9dd4ef9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Obsolete variables and code related to non-Fluent scrollbars on Windows have been removed from the public header renderer_preferences.h. Embedders relying on these preferences will be affected. (07c1a24)

Security

  • History-related services like HistoryTabHelper and HistoryClustersService are now correctly disabled in Incognito profiles to prevent crashes and potential data leaks. (a05d583)
  • The UserInterventionForPasswordChange feature, which prompts users before a site-initiated password change, is now enabled by default. (ea0cf30)
  • Input handling in InputRouterImpl and PassthroughTouchEventQueue was hardened against Use-After-Free vulnerabilities that could occur during event processing. (33e1063)
  • Media Mojo services were hardened to handle invalid input from untrusted clients by using mojo::ReportBadMessage() instead of NOTREACHED(), preventing potential host process crashes. (3fc8eee)
  • The UseUnexportableKeyServiceInBrowserProcess feature is now enabled by default on Windows to reduce OAuth multilogin errors. (94c5447)

APIs and Extensions

  • A new asynchronous API, GetScreenshotsForTabs, was added to SessionSyncService to allow retrieval of screenshots for synced tabs from other devices. (af20922)
  • The Web Speech API now integrates the TinyGemma audio model to improve on-device speech recognition quality, controlled by the kOnDeviceWebSpeechSmallExpertModel feature flag. (ccd0ea2)
  • The omnibox will now prefer built-in starter pack keywords (e.g., @gemini) over conflicting extension-defined keywords to prevent crashes and improve user experience. (3f38d35)
  • The observer notification order in ToolbarActionsModel was fixed to ensure OnToolbarActionRemoved is called before OnToolbarPinnedActionsChanged, preventing observers from referencing already-removed actions. (3d269c1)

Browser UI and Features

  • Backend infrastructure was added to support importing and exporting flag configurations from chrome://flags. (b973615)
  • A new context menu item, "Listen to this page," has been added for desktop platforms, enabled by the kImprovedReadAloud feature flag. (021e219)
  • A bug was fixed where pinned toolbar actions, like the download button, would not appear correctly in popup windows. (fa8e625)
  • On macOS, right-clicking on Top-Chrome WebUIs now correctly makes the view the first responder, fixing focus-related issues with menus. (5aae030)
  • A crash that could occur when abruptly closing the Profile Picker window while the Managed User Profile Notice was displayed has been resolved. (6d57626)

Core Internals and Testing

  • ProcessMap and its callers were updated to use the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId instead of raw integer IDs for improved type safety in process management. (ab62ab0)
  • A new interactive testing verb, MaybeEnterInteractiveMode(), was added to InteractiveViewsTest to allow developers to pause tests for UI inspection. (06548ff)
  • A thread-safety issue in base::FormatDouble was fixed by using base::ThreadLocalOwnedPointer to prevent data races. (4ee829d)
  • The persistent cache backend (SqliteBackendImpl) now provides more detailed error information on initialization failures, allowing callers to better handle database corruption or disk errors. (c426fc2)
  • Platform-independent source files for the Supervised User feature were moved from chrome/browser into a dedicated supervised_user component as part of a larger modularization effort. (6ba2a7b)

2026-04-26

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-26

Overview

This digest covers 17 commits, with 9 relevant to desktop browser development after filtering for platform and scope. The changes focus on UI performance, feature cleanup, and infrastructure improvements. No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified in this set of commits. The analysis ranges from commit (c708812) to (27ab255).

Browser UI & Features

  • The Omnibox popup's performance has been improved by deferring its display until visually ready and keeping its WebContents attached on hide to prevent renderer stalls. This behavior is controlled by new feature flags (kOmniboxAimDetachWebContentsOnHide, kOmniboxWebUIDetachWebContentsOnHide). The internal OmniboxPopupPresenterBase::ShouldDeferUntilVisualStateReady() method signature was changed from returning a bool to std::optional<base::TimeDelta> to simplify the interface. (c708812)
  • Minor cleanups and refactors were made to several browser features. The feature flag kIPHPlusAddressFirstSaveFeature for an In-Product Help bubble was removed. Internal code for the 'Send Tab to Self' feature was refactored for clarity. Additionally, metrics for NTP voice search were migrated to a unified component to support future deprecation of legacy code. (82f1866) (4901bca) (a2652ff)
  • Several minor UI tweaks were applied to the experimental 'Contextual Tasks' feature, including adjustments to composebox animations and CSS margins. (ecf2c1f) (27ab255)

Infrastructure & Tooling

  • The Windows installer logic for detecting duplicate file hierarchies (IsIdenticalFileHierarchy) has been improved with additional logging and a bug fix to correctly return false on enumeration errors. Separately, a performance refactor in the accessibility code (ui/accessibility) reduces unnecessary string copies by using string_view and base::JoinString. (9d390f2) (6de91e4)

2026-04-25

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-25

Overview

This digest covers 60 commits from the Chromium repository, with 18 selected for summary based on their relevance to browser APIs, extensions, networking, security, and UI for Windows and macOS. The changes span from commit (0ce5626) to (f334d29). No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified in this set.

Security & Privacy

  • The Geolocation service has been hardened to prevent a privacy leak where the 15-minute throttle on approximate locations could be bypassed. The throttle state is now persisted even when all clients disconnect, preventing attackers from churning Mojo connections to reconstruct a precise location. (b76dfb0)
  • A JSON injection and out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the Live Caption Translation feature was fixed. The issue stemmed from using base::StringPrintf to construct the request body, which has now been replaced with the safer base::WriteJson API to ensure proper escaping and bounds checking. (a1ab0fb)
  • Web App Manifest processing is now stricter, with the browser process enforcing same-origin checks for start_url, id, scope, and share_target->action fields to prevent cross-origin data leakage. (0b080ce)
  • On Windows, the Chrome Elevation Service has been updated to use an explicitly system-locked temporary directory via base::GetSecureTempDirectory() instead of the default C:\Windows\Temp, providing a defense-in-depth security improvement. (3297b1b)

GPU & Media

  • The GPU command decoder now rejects program relinks if transform feedback is active. This prevents state desynchronization between the GL driver and the command decoder, improving stability and security. (8617532)
  • A related fix ensures that transform feedback is correctly paused when switching to a newly created GL context, preventing state from being left on accidentally and causing rendering issues or instability. (f41b46e)
  • A race condition in the Windows D3D12 and Media Foundation hardware video encoders has been fixed. The change ensures that read access to a video frame is maintained for the entire duration of the encode, preventing potential concurrent write access and crashes. (28ec09a)

Networking

  • The WebTransport API has been updated to align with the W3C specification by adding a congestionControl hint. This new option in WebTransportOptions allows developers to suggest a preference for "throughput" (BBRv2), "low-latency" (BBRv1), or "default" (Cubic) congestion control algorithms. (7faefb2)

APIs & Services

  • A new utility class, base::i18n::LanguageCode, has been introduced to the base library for creating and manipulating BCP-47 language codes at compile time, providing a type-safe way to handle locale identifiers. (28c918a)
  • The on-device model service has been updated to pass a file descriptor for a GPU shader program cache via IPC. This change aims to reduce model setup time on subsequent runs by caching compiled shaders. (cdc476c)

UI & Accessibility

  • An accessibility bug has been fixed where renaming a tab group would not update the accessible name for tabs within that group. Now, UpdateAccessibleName() is called on each tab in the group, ensuring screen readers announce the correct, up-to-date group name. (fb35a0d)
  • A new feature flag, kEnergyEffectInOmnibox, has been added to control a new visual effect in the Omnibox, allowing it to be toggled for experiments or as a killswitch. (0b7dccd)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • A new test helper, content::ReadyForInputObserver, has been added to the public test API. This utility helps prevent flaky tests by allowing them to wait until a WebContents is fully ready to receive input before sending synthetic events. (4398d46)
  • As part of an ongoing effort to modernize test infrastructure, the SystemStorageApiTest and SocketsTcpServerApiTest for extensions have been migrated from the deprecated "app shell" environment to the standard chrome/browser_tests suite. (dc4393c) (0aeb02c)
  • The NotificationUIManagerTest has been refactored to remove its dependency on BrowserWithTestWindowTest, simplifying the test fixture and improving its isolation. (ceef2f8)
